chapters.title: 4. The Action Economy — How Combat Flows

sections.title: The GM Action Tracker
sections.content:
At the start of a scene, the GM places a number of **Fear tokens** on the Action Tracker based on the scene's complexity:

| Scene Type | Starting Fear | Examples |
| :--- | :---: | :--- |
| Incidental | 0-1 | A tense conversation that might turn violent; a minor customs inspection. |
| Minor | 1-3 | A bar fight in the Underbelly; a brief skirmish with pirates during docking. |
| Standard | 2-4 | A firefight in a station corridor; a tense standoff with Mindclave agents. |
| Major | 4-8 | A full boarding action; a battle with an Eclipse Void-Shape; a political assassination attempt. |
| Climactic | 6-12 | The final assault on the Eclipse Nexus; the defense of the Grand Assembly during a coup. |

The GM gains additional Fear tokens whenever a player rolls **Fear** on their action dice (the lower of the 2d12 is higher). The GM **spends** Fear tokens to activate adversary actions, trigger environmental hazards, or introduce complications.

sections.title: What a Player Can Do
sections.content:
When a player has the **spotlight**, they may do the following:

**One Action.** The player's primary contribution to the scene. This includes attacking, using a Domain card, attempting a skill check, providing aid to an ally, or interacting with the environment. The player describes what they want to do and rolls 2d12 + the relevant Trait.

**Movement.** The player may move one range band (e.g., from Far to Close, or from Close to Very Close) as part of their action. Moving through dangerous terrain or past enemies may require an additional roll.

**A Free Action.** Brief actions that do not require a roll, such as speaking a short sentence, drawing a weapon, or pressing a button. The GM determines what qualifies as a free action.

sections.title: Passing the Spotlight
sections.content: After a player resolves their action, the spotlight passes. On a **Hope result** (the higher d12 is the Hope die), the player chooses which ally receives the spotlight next. On a **Fear result** (the higher d12 is the Fear die), the GM gains a Fear token and decides who acts next — this could be another player or an adversary.

sections.title: GM Turns
sections.content:
The GM does not have a fixed turn. Instead, the GM **spends Fear tokens** to activate adversary actions at any point during the scene. Each adversary action costs Fear:

| Adversary Action | Fear Cost |
| :--- | :--- |
| Standard Attack (one adversary attacks one target) | 1 Fear |
| Move (one adversary moves one range band) | 0 Fear (free with an attack) |
| Special Ability (use a named ability from the adversary's stat block) | 1-3 Fear (listed on the stat block) |
| Environmental Hazard (trigger a trap, explosion, or structural collapse) | 1-2 Fear |
| Reinforcements (a new group of minions arrives) | 2-4 Fear |
| Escalation (the situation gets dramatically worse — alarms sound, a hull breach opens, a hostage is taken) | 3-5 Fear |

sections.title: Range Bands
sections.content:
*Nexus 9* uses abstract range bands rather than a grid. This keeps combat fast and cinematic.

| Range Band | Description | Sci-Fi Examples |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Melee** | Within arm's reach. | Grappling in a corridor; fighting with a shock-pike. |
| **Very Close** | A few steps away; the same room. | Across a small cargo bay; opposite sides of an airlock. |
| **Close** | Across a large room or down a corridor. | Across the Promenade marketplace; down a station spoke. |
| **Far** | Across a plaza or a large open area. | Across a docking bay; from one end of a habitat ring to the other. |
| **Very Far** | At the edge of visibility or sensor range. | Across the exterior hull; sniper positions on a different ring. |

---

chapters.content: *Nexus 9* does not use traditional initiative. Instead, combat uses a **fluid spotlight system** driven by the **Hope and Fear economy**.

sections.title: Stress
sections.content:
**Stress** represents mental and emotional strain — fear, exhaustion, psychic pressure, and moral compromise. Characters mark Stress when they:

- Take a Major or Severe hit (as described in the Damage Threshold rules above)
- Use certain powerful Domain cards or telepathic abilities
- Witness something horrifying or morally devastating
- Push themselves beyond their limits (e.g., sprinting through a firefight, staying awake for days)

When a character **marks their last Stress slot**, they reach their **Breaking Point**. They must immediately choose one of the following:

| Breaking Point Option | Effect |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Lash Out** | The character acts recklessly. They gain Advantage on their next attack but all enemies gain Advantage on attacks against them until the end of the scene. |
| **Shut Down** | The character freezes or withdraws. They cannot take actions until an ally spends 1 Hope to snap them out of it, or until the scene ends. |
| **Compromise** | The character makes a desperate bargain — they agree to something they would normally refuse, reveal a secret, or abandon an objective. The GM and player negotiate the terms. |

After reaching a Breaking Point, the character **clears half their Stress slots** (rounded up) and continues.

sections.title: Conditions
sections.content:
Conditions are specific debuffs applied by adversary abilities, environmental hazards, or failed rolls.

| Condition | Effect | Clearing |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Pinned Down** | You cannot move from your current position. You have Disadvantage on attack rolls. | Spend 1 Hope, or an ally provides covering fire (uses their action). |
| **Blinded** | You have Disadvantage on all rolls that require sight. You cannot make attacks beyond Very Close range. | Clears at the end of the scene, or when the source is removed. |
| **Exposed** | Your Evasion is reduced by 2. Enemies have Advantage on attacks against you. | Move to cover (requires movement), or an ally uses Shield-Bearer or equivalent. |
| **Restrained** | You cannot move. You have Disadvantage on Agility and Finesse rolls. | Succeed on a Strength roll (Difficulty 13), or an ally frees you. |
| **Disoriented** | You have Disadvantage on Knowledge and Instinct rolls. You cannot use telepathic abilities. | Clears at the end of your next action, or when an ally spends 1 Hope. |
| **Wounded** | Your damage thresholds are reduced by 2 at all levels. This condition persists until you take a long rest or receive medical treatment. | Long rest, or a Medic uses Combat Triage or equivalent. |
| **Compromised** | Your communications are being monitored. Any plans you discuss openly are known to the enemy. | Succeed on a Knowledge roll (Difficulty 15) to sweep for bugs, or change your comm frequency (requires an Engineer). |

---

sections.title: Short Rest
sections.content:
A **Short Rest** represents a brief pause — catching your breath behind cover, patching a wound in a maintenance corridor, or regrouping in a safe room. A Short Rest takes approximately **30 minutes to 1 hour** of in-game time.

During a Short Rest, each character may:

- **Clear up to half their marked Stress slots** (rounded up).
- **Clear 1 HP** (representing basic first aid and adrenaline recovery).
- **Clear all Armor Slots** (representing field repairs to armor and equipment).
- **Perform one Short Rest action** from the following list:

| Short Rest Action | Description |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Field Repair** | Repair a damaged piece of equipment or weapon. Requires an Engineer or appropriate tools. |
| **Patch Up** | A Medic can heal an ally for an additional 1d6 HP. |
| **Gather Intel** | Make a Knowledge or Instinct roll to analyze the situation and gain information about the next encounter. |
| **Secure the Area** | Make an Instinct roll to set up a defensive position, granting +1 Evasion to all allies in the area for the next encounter. |
| **Recall a Domain Card** | Pay the Recall Cost (in Hope) to retrieve a Domain card from your Vault and add it to your active Loadout. |

sections.title: Long Rest
sections.content:
A **Long Rest** represents a full recovery period — sleeping in quarters, receiving proper medical treatment in a med-bay, or spending a full day in port. A Long Rest takes approximately **8 hours** of in-game time and requires a safe location.

During a Long Rest, each character:

- **Clears all HP.**
- **Clears all Stress.**
- **Clears all Armor Slots.**
- **Clears all Conditions** (except permanent injuries or scars).
- **May perform two Downtime Moves** from the following list:

| Downtime Move | Description |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Work a Contact** | Reach out to an NPC in your network. Make a Presence roll to gain a favor, a piece of information, or a lead. |
| **Forge Documents** | Create false identification, transit papers, or diplomatic credentials. Requires a Finesse roll. |
| **Decrypt Data** | Analyze a data chip, intercepted transmission, or encrypted file. Requires a Knowledge roll. |
| **Upgrade Equipment** | Spend Credits to improve a weapon or piece of armor to the next tier (requires appropriate facilities and an Engineer). |
| **Synthesize Compounds** | A Medic can craft medical supplies (Combat Stims, Antidotes, Neural Stabilizers) using available materials. |
| **Train** | Practice a specific skill. Gain a +1 bonus to your next roll using a specific Trait (this bonus is consumed after one use). |
| **Repair the Ship** | Clear System Stress from the crew's ship. Requires an Engineer and appropriate facilities. |
| **Investigate the Station** | Explore a specific area of Nexus 9. The GM provides a discovery, a rumor, or a complication based on the area explored. |
| **Lay Low** | Reduce your visibility. If any faction is actively searching for you, their progress is delayed by one step on their Threat Clock. |
| **Carouse** | Spend time in the Promenade or Underbelly. Make a Presence roll — on a success, you hear a useful rumor or make a new contact. On a failure, you attract unwanted attention. |

---

chapters.title: 7. Death and Dying

sections.title: Scars
sections.content:
Scars are permanent consequences of near-death experiences. They are primarily narrative, but the GM and player should agree on a minor mechanical effect that reflects the scar. Examples:

| Scar | Narrative Effect | Mechanical Effect |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Lost Eye | Depth perception is compromised. | -1 to ranged attack rolls beyond Far range. |
| Cybernetic Arm | A mechanical replacement for a lost limb. | +1 to Strength rolls involving that arm; -1 to Finesse rolls requiring fine motor control. |
| Psychic Scarring | A telepathic attack left lasting damage. | -1 to resist telepathic intrusion; +1 to detect telepathic activity (you recognize the sensation). |
| Survivor's Guilt | You lost someone you could not save. | -1 to rolls when an ally is dying; +1 to rolls to protect allies from harm. |
| Respiratory Damage | Exposure to vacuum or toxic atmosphere. | Disadvantage on rolls requiring sustained physical exertion in low-oxygen environments. |

---

chapters.content:
When a character **marks their last HP**, they fall **unconscious** and begin dying. On each subsequent GM turn (or when the GM spends 1 Fear), the dying character must roll a **Death Move**:

**Roll 2d12.**

| Result | Outcome |
| :--- | :--- |
| **19+** | You stabilize. You are unconscious but no longer dying. You regain 1 HP when the scene ends. |
| **12-18** | You cling to life. Nothing changes. You must roll again on the next GM turn. |
| **6-11** | You slip closer to death. Mark 1 Stress (even though you are unconscious). If you have no Stress slots remaining, you die. |
| **2-5** | You are on the edge. You gain a **Scar** (a permanent narrative mark — a missing eye, a cybernetic replacement, a recurring nightmare, a phobia). You stabilize but are permanently changed. |

An ally can attempt to **stabilize** a dying character by spending their action and succeeding on a Knowledge roll (Difficulty 13). A Medic gains Advantage on this roll. Success stabilizes the character at 1 HP.

chapters.title: 8. Ship-Scale Damage Thresholds
chapters.content:
Starship combat uses the same threshold system as personal combat, but at a larger scale. Ship damage thresholds are determined by the ship's **Frame Class**, not by the crew.

| Frame Class | Hull Points | Minor | Major | Severe | System Stress Slots |
| :--- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| **Fighter** | 8-10 | 3 | 6 | 12 | 4 |
| **Courier** | 15-20 | 5 | 10 | 18 | 6 |
| **Freighter** | 25-30 | 7 | 14 | 25 | 8 |
| **Gunship** | 35-40 | 9 | 18 | 32 | 8 |
| **Cruiser** | 60-80 | 12 | 25 | 44 | 10 |
| **Capital** | 100-120 | 18 | 36 | 63 | 12 |

When a ship's **System Stress** is maxed out, the GM rolls on the **Critical System Failure Table**:

| Roll (1d12) | Critical System Failure |
| :---: | :--- |
| 1-2 | **Weapons Offline.** All weapon systems are disabled until an Engineer succeeds on a Knowledge roll (Difficulty 15) to restore them. |
| 3-4 | **Engines Stall.** The ship cannot move until an Engineer succeeds on a Knowledge roll (Difficulty 13) to restart the engines. The ship is a sitting target. |
| 5-6 | **Shields Collapse.** The ship's damage thresholds are halved (round down) until an Engineer reroutes power (requires 2 actions). |
| 7-8 | **Hull Breach.** All characters in the affected section must succeed on an Agility roll (Difficulty 13) or be Exposed. The breach must be sealed (Engineer action, Difficulty 15) or the section is vented to space in 3 rounds. |
| 9-10 | **Life Support Failing.** All characters on the ship begin marking 1 Stress per round until an Engineer restores life support (Difficulty 17). |
| 11 | **Navigation Offline.** The ship cannot make hyperspace jumps or use advanced sensors until repaired (Engineer, Difficulty 15). If in hyperspace, the ship drops out at a random location. |
| 12 | **Catastrophic Failure.** The ship is crippled. All systems go offline simultaneously. The crew has 1d4 rounds to abandon ship or attempt an emergency repair (Engineer, Difficulty 20). Failure means the ship is destroyed. |

---

chapters.title: Appendix A: Quick Reference — Damage Resolution
chapters.content:
**Step 1:** The attacker rolls their weapon's damage dice (e.g., 1d8+3 for a Plasma Carbine).

**Step 2:** Compare the total damage to the target's Damage Thresholds (from their armor, or their base class thresholds if unarmored).

**Step 3:** Determine the severity:

| Damage Total vs. Thresholds | HP Lost | Stress Marked |
| :--- | :---: | :---: |
| Below Minor | 0 HP | 0 Stress |
| Meets/Exceeds Minor | 1 HP | 0 Stress |
| Meets/Exceeds Major | 2 HP | 1 Stress |
| Meets/Exceeds Severe | 3 HP | 2 Stress |

**Step 4:** The target may **mark an Armor Slot** to reduce the severity by one step (e.g., Major becomes Minor: 1 HP instead of 2, no Stress).

**Step 5:** Apply the damage. If the target's last HP is marked, they fall unconscious and begin dying.

---

*End of Phase 1 Deliverable*

chapters.content:
**Version:** 1.0 Draft
**Date:** April 4, 2026
**Status:** Ready for Playtest

---

chapters.title: 1. Weapon Systems Overview
chapters.content:
Weapons in *Nexus 9* are categorized by the primary **Trait** used to wield them effectively. Every weapon also specifies a **Range**, a **Damage** value (rolled on a successful attack), a **Burden** (One-Handed or Two-Handed), and an optional **Feature** that modifies how the weapon behaves in combat.

The six Traits govern which weapons a character naturally favors:

| Trait | Weapon Archetype | Examples |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Agility** | Precision firearms, balanced melee blades | Mag-Pistol, Vibro-Sword, Sniper Rifle |
| **Strength** | Heavy ordnance, breaching tools, massive melee weapons | Gravity Hammer, Breaching Axe, Boarding Halberd |
| **Finesse** | Concealable sidearms, sniper systems, tactical blades | Holdout Blaster, Concealed Shiv, Hand Cannon |
| **Instinct** | Scatterguns, survival tools, intuitive tech | Scattergun, Survival Staff, Twin Blasters |
| **Presence** | Officer sidearms, dueling blades, intimidation weapons | Ceremonial Rapier, Officer's Cutlass, Diplomat's Scepter |
| **Knowledge** | Experimental tech, directed energy weapons, hacking tools | Hacking Wand, Heavy Plasma Caster, Fusion Gloves |

Damage types are broadly categorized as **Kinetic** (slugs, blades, explosives) or **Energy** (plasma, laser, shock, sonic). Some armors or adversaries may have specific resistances or vulnerabilities to one type. When a weapon deals "kinetic or energy" damage, the attacker chooses which type to apply on each successful attack.

sections.title: Carrying Capacity (Burden)
sections.content:
Characters in *Nexus 9* are limited by what they can reasonably carry into a firefight or diplomatic negotiation. This is represented by **Burden**.

**Weapons:** A character can carry a maximum of **two hands' worth** of weapons ready for immediate use (e.g., one Two-Handed weapon, or one One-Handed primary weapon and one One-Handed secondary weapon). They may carry one additional weapon stowed on their back or in a holster, which requires a Free Action to draw.

**Armor:** A character can only wear one suit of armor at a time.

**Consumables and Gear:** A character can carry up to **5 Consumables** and **3 pieces of Utility Gear** on their person without penalty. Carrying more requires a cargo bag, which imposes a -1 penalty to Agility rolls while worn.

sections.title: Acquiring Gear (The Credit Economy)
sections.content:
*Nexus 9* uses a simplified wealth system based on **Credits**. Instead of tracking individual coins, wealth is abstracted into tiers of purchasing power.

| Wealth Tier | Value | Examples |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Handful of Credits** | Pocket change | A meal, a cheap drink, a bribe to a dock worker, a single consumable |
| **Bag of Credits** | Moderate wealth | A night in a decent hotel, a Tier 1 weapon, a significant bribe, a shuttle ticket |
| **Chest of Credits** | Serious money | A Tier 3 weapon, a rare artifact, a small starship, a political favor |

chapters.title: Chapter 13: Starship Rules and Travel

sections.title: Ship Mechanics
sections.content:
*   **Hull Points (HP):** Represents the ship's physical integrity. When a ship reaches 0 HP, it is crippled, adrift, and vulnerable to boarding actions.
*   **System Stress:** Represents the strain on the ship's engines, shields, and life support. When a ship maxes out its System Stress, a critical system fails (e.g., weapons go offline, life support drops, or engines stall).
*   **Evasion:** The target number an attacker must roll to hit the ship, modified by the Pilot's Agility.

sections.title: Crew Roles and Actions
sections.content:
During starship combat, each player assumes a specific role on the ship. Each role has access to unique actions that contribute to the ship's survival and success.

*   **Helm (Pilot):** Controls the ship's movement. Can take the *Evasive Maneuvers* action (Agility roll) to increase the ship's Evasion for the round, or the *Pursuit* action to close the distance with a fleeing enemy.
*   **Tactical (Marine):** Controls the ship's weapons. Can take the *Fire Weapons* action (Finesse roll) to attack an enemy ship, or the *Target Lock* action to gain Advantage on the next attack.
*   **Engineering (Engineer):** Manages power and repairs. Can take the *Reroute Power* action (Knowledge roll) to clear 1 System Stress, or the *Emergency Repairs* action to restore 1d4 Hull Points.
*   **Sensors/Comms (Envoy/Mystic):** Manages information and electronic warfare. Can take the *Sensor Sweep* action (Instinct roll) to identify enemy weaknesses, or the *Jamming* action to impose Disadvantage on enemy attacks.

---

chapters.content: Starships in *Nexus 9* are treated as characters in their own right, with their own stats, modules, and damage tracks. Ship combat is designed to involve the entire crew, not just the pilot.

sections.title: Exploration Procedures
sections.content:
When the crew ventures into uncharted space or explores a derelict vessel, the GM uses Exploration Procedures to structure the tension.

*   **The Survey Roll:** Upon entering a new sector or boarding a derelict, the Sensor Operator (or any character with high Instinct) makes a Survey Roll. Success reveals the layout, life signs, and immediate hazards. Failure means the crew is ambushed or triggers a trap.
*   **The Hazard Die:** For every 10 minutes of in-game time spent exploring a dangerous environment, the GM rolls a d6. On a 1, a complication occurs (e.g., life support fails, a patrol spots them, or an environmental hazard activates).
*   **Salvage:** Exploring derelicts often yields valuable salvage. A successful Knowledge or Finesse roll allows a character to extract 1d10 x 100 Credits worth of usable parts or data.

sections.title: Ship Repair Rules
sections.content:
A crippled ship (0 HP) or a ship with maxed-out System Stress is a sitting duck. Repairs require time, resources, and skill.

*   **Emergency Repairs (In Combat):** The Engineer can take the *Emergency Repairs* action (Knowledge roll) to restore 1d4 Hull Points or clear 1 System Stress. This requires an Action Token and represents jury-rigging systems to keep the ship flying.
*   **Field Repairs (Out of Combat):** If the ship is safe but damaged, the crew can spend a short rest (1 hour) conducting field repairs. A successful Knowledge roll restores 1d8 Hull Points and clears all System Stress. This consumes 1 unit of Spare Parts (costing 500 Credits).
*   **Drydock Repairs:** Major structural damage requires a drydock facility (like those on Nexus 9). Drydock repairs cost 1,000 Credits per Hull Point restored and take 1 day per point.

---

|:---:|:---|
| **1** | A street vendor is selling "genuine" Progenitor artifacts. A Knowledge roll reveals they are cheap fakes, but one item in the pile is real. |
| **2** | A brawl breaks out between off-duty Terran Marines and Kaelen Syndicate thugs. Coalition Security is nowhere to be seen. |
| **3** | A pickpocket bumps into a PC. Make an Instinct roll to notice the theft of 1d10 x 10 Credits or a minor item. |
| **4** | A holographic advertisement malfunctions, looping a subliminal Mindclave recruitment message. |
| **5** | A smuggler from the Underbelly offers the crew a highly illegal weapon modification at a steep discount, but they need the Credits *now*. |
| **6** | A sudden power surge plunges the Promenade into darkness for two minutes. When the lights return, someone is dead. |
| **7** | A Valari Warrior Caste member is challenging passersby to honorable non-lethal combat to test their worth. |
| **8** | A frantic refugee begs the crew for help, claiming they are being hunted by a Bounty Hunter (Tier 2 Skulk). |
| **9** | A high-stakes gambling game is taking place in a VIP lounge. The buy-in is steep, but the information being traded is priceless. |
| **10** | A street preacher is screaming about the return of Aethelgard and the end of the cycle, drawing a large, agitated crowd. |
| **11** | A Coalition Security patrol is conducting random, aggressive "stop and frisk" searches, targeting non-humans. |
| **12** | A Syndicate Fixer (Tier 1 Support) approaches the crew with a job offer that sounds too good to be true. |

sections.title: The Red Ring (Military/Security)
sections.content:
*Atmosphere: Gun oil, antiseptic, harsh lighting, 1.1g gravity.*

| d12 | Encounter |
|:---:|:---|
| **1** | A squad of Terran Marines (Tier 1 Standard) is conducting a live-fire drill in a restricted corridor. |
| **2** | A Coalition Security Officer (Tier 1 Standard) demands to see the crew's identification and travel permits. |
| **3** | A massive Aurelian Imperial Dreadnought docks at Bay Alpha, its weapons visibly powered up. |
| **4** | A prisoner transfer goes wrong. A Syndicate Breacher (Tier 2 Bruiser) breaks their restraints and attacks the guards. |
| **5** | A low-level radiation leak (Hazard) triggers an automated lockdown, trapping the crew in a corridor for an hour. |
| **6** | A group of mechanics is arguing over how to repair a damaged jump engine. A PC with high Knowledge could help and earn a favor. |
| **7** | A Vanguard operative is caught trying to slice into the station's primary sensor array. |
| **8** | A Kaelen Syndicate smuggling ship is impounded, and the crew is watching from the shadows, planning a heist to get their cargo back. |
| **9** | Commander Vance is personally inspecting the docking bays, looking exhausted and angry. |
| **10** | A shipment of military-grade combat stims is left unguarded for exactly two minutes. |
| **11** | A Mindclave Inquisitor (Tier 2 Support) is interrogating a terrified dock worker. |
| **12** | An automated Station Security Drone (Tier 1 Ranged) malfunctions and targets the crew, classifying them as "hostile intruders." |

sections.title: The Gold Ring (Scientific)
sections.content:
*Atmosphere: Sterile scrubbers, chemical preservatives, bright clinical lighting.*

| d12 | Encounter |
|:---:|:---|
| **1** | A containment breach in the Xenobiology Lab! A Valari Bio-Construct (Tier 2 Bruiser) is loose in the corridors. |
| **2** | A researcher frantically asks the crew to help them transport a volatile chemical compound before it degrades. |
| **3** | A Mindclave telepath is undergoing a painful, experimental procedure to increase their P-rating. |
| **4** | A group of scientists is debating the ethical implications of reverse-engineering Eclipse crystalline technology. |
| **5** | A medical emergency requires a PC with high Finesse or Knowledge to assist in a life-saving surgery. |
| **6** | A strange, localized temporal anomaly causes the crew to experience the same three minutes twice. |
| **7** | A researcher offers to pay the crew handsomely if they will test an experimental combat stim (with unknown side effects). |
| **8** | A Terran Combat Medic (Tier 1 Support) is stealing supplies to treat refugees in the Grey Ring. |
| **9** | A Valari priest is arguing with a scientist over the dissection of a creature they consider sacred. |
| **10** | A data-terminal is left logged in, displaying classified research on the Nexus Core. |
| **11** | A sudden drop in temperature and a feeling of profound dread indicates the presence of a Void-Spawn (Tier 1 Minion) hiding in the vents. |
| **12** | An explosion rocks a nearby lab. The crew must navigate fire and toxic smoke to rescue the survivors. |

sections.title: The Grey Ring (Industrial/Residential)
sections.content:
*Atmosphere: Sweat, rust, recycled water, erratic lighting, inconsistent gravity.*

| d12 | Encounter |
|:---:|:---|
| **1** | A gang of Underbelly Thugs (Tier 1 Minions) attempts to shake down the crew for a "transit toll." |
| **2** | A localized gravity failure (Hazard) sends heavy cargo crates floating dangerously through the air. |
| **3** | A black-market auction is taking place, selling stolen military hardware and illegal cybernetics. |
| **4** | A group of refugees is huddled around a failing atmospheric scrubber, struggling to breathe. |
| **5** | A Syndicate "Cleaner" (Tier 3 Skulk) is disposing of a body in a waste reclamation vat. |
| **6** | A secret Vanguard meeting is interrupted by a Coalition Security raid. |
| **7** | A cult dedicated to the Eclipse is holding a dark ritual in an abandoned cargo bay. |
| **8** | A massive, jury-rigged machine explodes, causing a hull breach (Hazard) that must be sealed immediately. |
| **9** | A child steals a vital piece of equipment from a PC and darts into a maze of narrow ventilation shafts. |
| **10** | A turf war breaks out between two rival smuggling gangs, catching the crew in the crossfire. |
| **11** | A rumor spreads that the entrance to Grey Level 17 has been found, and a mob is forming to explore it. |
| **12** | A Whisperer in the Dark (Tier 3 Skulk) stalks the crew through a section of unlit, abandoned corridors. |

chapters.content: When the crew is moving through the station, the GM can roll a **d12** on the appropriate table to generate a random encounter. These encounters are not always combat; many are social, environmental, or atmospheric.

chapters.title: 2. Off-Station Exploration Procedures

sections.title: The Exploration Turn
sections.content:
Exploration is divided into **Turns**, representing roughly 4-6 hours of travel, scanning, or deep-space maneuvering. During each turn, the crew must assign roles (Helm, Sensors, Engineering) and make a **Survey Roll**.

**The Survey Roll:** The Sensor Operator (or any character with high Instinct) rolls 2d12 + Instinct against a Difficulty set by the environment (usually 12 for known space, 15 for the Frontier, 18 for the Rimward Marches).

*   **Success with Hope:** The crew discovers something valuable (salvage, a hidden route, a tactical advantage) and avoids any hazards.
*   **Success with Fear:** The crew navigates safely, but the GM gains 1 Fear or a complication arises (e.g., a minor system failure, a delayed arrival).
*   **Failure with Hope:** The crew encounters a hazard or enemy, but they have the upper hand or a chance to avoid it.
*   **Failure with Fear:** The crew is ambushed, suffers a major system failure, or blunders into a deadly hazard.

sections.title: Deep Space Encounter Table
sections.content:
If an encounter is triggered during an Exploration Turn, roll a **d12**:

| d12 | Deep Space Encounter |
|:---:|:---|
| **1** | **Derelict Ship:** A centuries-old Elder vessel drifting without power. High salvage potential, but likely guarded by automated defenses or Void-Spawn. |
| **2** | **Pirate Ambush:** A Kaelen Syndicate or Independent pirate crew (Tier 2) attacks from the cover of an asteroid field. |
| **3** | **Distress Signal:** A civilian transport is under attack or suffering a critical failure. Rescuing them earns major faction reputation. |
| **4** | **Hyperspace Anomaly:** A localized tear in space-time. The Helm must make a Difficulty 15 Agility roll to avoid taking 1d6 Hull Damage. |
| **5** | **Aurelian Patrol:** An Imperial cruiser demands to board and inspect the crew's ship for "contraband." |
| **6** | **Progenitor Relic:** A massive, geometric structure floating in the void. Approaching it causes strange telepathic whispers and visions. |
| **7** | **Silicate Swarm:** A cloud of crystalline, ship-eating organisms (Tier 2 Minions) descends on the vessel. |
| **8** | **Terran Blockade:** A Coalition fleet has locked down a hyperspace gate, demanding exorbitant tolls or bribes to pass. |
| **9** | **Technoseer Outpost:** A hidden, cloaked station. The Technoseers offer rare technology in exchange for dangerous favors. |
| **10** | **Asteroid Field:** A dense field of debris. The Helm must navigate carefully, or Engineering must constantly repair minor hull breaches. |
| **11** | **Mindclave Black Site:** A heavily guarded, off-the-books prison facility. Approaching too closely triggers a psychic warning to turn back. |
| **12** | **Hyperspace Phantom:** A terrifying, incorporeal entity (Tier 3 Solo) stalks the ship while it is in hyperspace, attempting to drive the crew mad. |

---

chapters.content: When the crew leaves Nexus 9 to explore the galaxy, the GM uses the **Exploration Procedure** to generate content and manage pacing.

chapters.title: 3. The Station Event Generator

sections.title: Rolling a Station Event
sections.content:
Roll **1d12** to determine the category of the event, then roll **1d6** to determine the specific occurrence.

#### 1-3: Economic & Infrastructure Events
| d6 | Event | Effect on Play |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| **1** | **Supply Chain Disruption:** A major freighter convoy is delayed by Kaelen pirates. | Prices for all consumables and gear increase by 50% for the session. |
| **2** | **Docking Bay Strike:** The Stevedores' Union in the Red Ring goes on strike over hazardous working conditions. | The crew cannot access their ship or heavy cargo without negotiating, bribing, or sneaking past the picket line. |
| **3** | **Power Grid Fluctuation:** The Nexus Core experiences a micro-surge, causing rolling blackouts in the Grey and Green Rings. | All Agility and Finesse rolls in affected areas suffer Disadvantage due to poor lighting and failing gravity plating. |
| **4** | **Black Market Crackdown:** Commander Vance orders a massive sweep of the Underbelly. | Finding illegal goods or contacts requires a Difficulty 16 Street Smarts roll. Failure results in Coalition Security questioning. |
| **5** | **Tech Boom:** A new shipment of salvaged Progenitor tech floods the market. | The crew can purchase one Tier-appropriate Relic or Ship Upgrade at a 25% discount, but it may be defective or tracked. |
| **6** | **Quarantine Protocol:** A strange, highly contagious pathogen is detected in the Gold Ring. | The Gold Ring is sealed. Anyone entering or leaving must pass a Difficulty 15 Knowledge (Medicine) check or become infected (take 1 Stress per hour). |

#### 4-6: Diplomatic & Political Events
| d6 | Event | Effect on Play |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| **1** | **Aurelian State Visit:** A high-ranking Imperial Duke arrives, demanding luxury accommodations and absolute deference. | The Blue and Green Rings are flooded with Imperial Guards. Aurelian NPCs have Advantage on all social rolls. |
| **2** | **Valari Holy Day:** The station's Valari population observes a day of fasting and silent meditation. | Valari NPCs will not engage in commerce or combat unless attacked. The station is eerily quiet. |
| **3** | **Mindclave Audit:** Director Thorne initiates a station-wide "loyalty assessment" for all Terran Coalition personnel. | Terran NPCs are paranoid and uncooperative. Any telepathic activity draws immediate Mindclave attention. |
| **4** | **League Summit:** The League of Unaligned Worlds holds a chaotic, multi-day voting session in the Grand Assembly Hall. | The Blue Ring is impassable due to protests and security cordons. Rumors and misinformation run rampant. |
| **5** | **Syndicate Turf War:** Two rival Kaelen crime families openly clash in the Promenade. | The Green Ring is a war zone. Moving through it requires a Difficulty 14 Agility or Instinct roll to avoid stray crossfire (1d8 damage). |
| **6** | **Vanguard Leak:** A classified Vanguard dossier is leaked to the public, exposing a major faction's secret operations. | Choose one faction. Their reputation plummets, and their agents are actively hunted by rivals for the duration of the session. |

#### 7-9: Environmental & Cosmic Events
| d6 | Event | Effect on Play |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| **1** | **Solar Flare:** The local star emits a massive burst of radiation, interfering with station shielding. | All communications (commlinks, datapads) are scrambled. The crew must rely on face-to-face contact. |
| **2** | **Hyperspace Wake:** A massive, unidentified vessel drops out of hyperspace near the station, leaving a turbulent wake. | All ships attempting to dock or launch suffer Disadvantage on Piloting rolls. |
| **3** | **Micro-Meteoroid Shower:** A cloud of debris strikes the station's outer hull, causing localized breaches. | The Grey Ring experiences sudden decompression events. The crew must wear environmental suits or risk suffocation. |
| **4** | **Psychic Resonance:** A wave of unexplained telepathic static washes over the station, causing nightmares and headaches. | All characters (especially Mystics) start the session with 1 Stress. Telepathic abilities cost an additional 1 Stress to use. |
| **5** | **The Whisperer's Shadow:** The Eclipse-Touched are active. A feeling of profound dread settles over a specific sector. | Choose one location. All Fear rolls made in that location generate 2 Fear instead of 1. |
| **6** | **Temporal Echo:** The station passes through a minor time-rift. | For one hour, characters experience intense déjà vu. Once per session, a player can force the GM to reroll a single die, claiming they "saw it happen already." |

#### 10-12: The Rumor Mill (Plot Hooks)
| d6 | Event | Effect on Play |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| **1** | **The Dead Drop:** A panicked informant shoves a data-chit into a PC's hand and runs, only to be gunned down moments later. | The chit contains coordinates to a hidden Eclipse cache or a corrupt official's blackmail file. |
| **2** | **The Missing Ship:** A Terran Coalition freighter vanished en route to Nexus 9, carrying vital medical supplies. | The crew is offered a bounty to find the ship. It was taken by Technoseers or Deep Space Horrors. |
| **3** | **The Defector:** A high-ranking Mindclave Hound is seeking asylum in the Valari embassy. | Both factions hire the crew—one to extract the defector, the other to silence them. |
| **4** | **The Ancient Signal:** The Nexus Core begins broadcasting a repeating, mathematical sequence that no one can decipher. | The crew must find a Progenitor Relic or a Technoseer to translate the signal before it attracts unwanted attention. |
| **5** | **The Smuggler's Debt:** Jax (or another Underbelly contact) calls in a favor. They need the crew to move a "hot" package off-station immediately. | The package is a living, breathing political prisoner or a dormant Eclipse-Corrupted entity. |
| **6** | **The Ghost of Aethelgard:** Sightings of a figure matching Aethelgard's description are reported in Grey Level 17. | The Vanguard mobilizes to investigate, but it might be a trap set by The Whisperer. |

---

chapters.content:
Nexus 9 is a city of millions, and it does not wait for the players to act. To make the station feel alive, the GM should roll on the **Station Event Generator** at the start of every session, or whenever the crew returns to the station after a long absence.

These events provide background flavor, alter the station's economy, or create immediate complications for the crew.

chapters.title: 4. The Faction Turn System

sections.title: Faction Attributes
sections.content:
Each major faction has three attributes, rated from 1 to 5:
*   **Force:** Military might, fleet size, and raw combat capability.
*   **Cunning:** Espionage, telepathy, sabotage, and hidden influence.
*   **Wealth:** Economic power, trade control, and diplomatic leverage.

sections.title: Starting Faction Ratings
sections.content:
| Faction | Force | Cunning | Wealth | Current Goal |
|:---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---|
| **Terran Coalition** | 4 | 2 | 4 | Secure the Nexus Core; contain the Kaelen. |
| **The Mindclave** | 2 | 5 | 3 | Infiltrate the Aurelian court; locate rogue telepaths. |
| **The Valari** | 4 | 3 | 2 | Protect the secret of the surrender; find the lost crystals. |
| **Aurelian Empire** | 3 | 4 | 5 | Reclaim border colonies; acquire Eclipse technology. |
| **Kaelen Syndicate** | 3 | 3 | 2 | Expand smuggling routes; provoke the Aurelians. |
| **The Vanguard** | 1 | 5 | 1 | Expose The Whisperer; protect the crew. |
| **The Eclipse** | 5 | 5 | 1 | Awaken the dormant fleets; corrupt the station leadership. |

sections.title: Running the Faction Turn
sections.content:
During a Faction Turn, the GM selects **two factions** that are currently active in the narrative. For each faction, the GM chooses one of their attributes (Force, Cunning, or Wealth) to advance their Current Goal.

The GM then rolls **1d10** and adds the chosen attribute rating.

#### Faction Action Results

| Roll Total | Result | Narrative Effect |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| **1-5** | **Disaster** | The faction's plan fails spectacularly. They lose 1 point in the chosen attribute (minimum 1) and suffer a major public setback. The players hear about this failure on the news feeds. |
| **6-9** | **Stalemate** | The faction expends resources but achieves nothing. Their plan is blocked by a rival faction or unforeseen circumstances. No attribute changes. |
| **10-13** | **Success** | The faction achieves their goal. They gain a minor advantage in the sector (e.g., a new trade route, a successful assassination, a border skirmish won). The players feel the ripple effects. |
| **14+** | **Dominance** | The faction achieves a massive victory. They permanently increase one attribute of their choice by 1 (maximum 5) and significantly alter the balance of power on Nexus 9. |

sections.title: Player Interference
sections.content:
The players' actions during the campaign directly impact the Faction Turn.
*   If the players actively **helped** a faction achieve its goal during the previous chapter, that faction gains **Advantage** (roll 2d10, take the highest) on their Faction Turn roll.
*   If the players actively **hindered** or sabotaged a faction, that faction suffers **Disadvantage** (roll 2d10, take the lowest) on their Faction Turn roll.

sections.title: Example Faction Turn
sections.content:
*The GM decides the Aurelian Empire is trying to use its Wealth (5) to bribe key officials in the Green Ring to overlook their smuggling of Eclipse artifacts. The players did not interfere with this plot recently.*

*The GM rolls 1d10 and gets a 6. Adding the Aurelian Wealth of 5, the total is 11 (Success).*

*Narrative Effect: The Aurelians successfully bribe the dockmasters. In the next session, the players will notice Aurelian ships bypassing customs, and Eclipse-Corrupted enemies will become more common in the Green Ring.*

chapters.content:
In *Nexus 9*, the major powers do not sit idle while the players adventure. To simulate the shifting political landscape, the GM should run a **Faction Turn** at the end of every campaign chapter (or roughly every 3-4 sessions).

This system uses a simplified abstraction of faction resources and goals to determine what happens off-screen.

chapters.title: 5. Using These Tools in Play
chapters.content:
These procedural tools are designed to reduce GM prep time and ensure that Nexus 9 feels like a living, dangerous place.
*   Use the **Ring Encounter Tables** when the players are traveling between locations or investigating a new area.
*   Use the **Station Event Generator** at the start of a session to set the tone and provide immediate complications.
*   Use the **Exploration Procedures** when the crew leaves the station to explore derelicts, the Frontier, or the Rimward Marches.
*   Use the **Faction Turn System** between major story arcs to update the political landscape and generate new plot hooks based on the factions' successes and failures.

---

*End of GM Procedural Tools*

books.title: BOOK SIX: THREATS FROM THE DARK

chapters.title: Adversaries & Threats Guide
chapters.content:
**Version:** 3.0 (52 Stat Blocks)
**Date:** April 4, 2026
**Status:** Ready for Playtest

---

chapters.title: 1. The Kaelen Syndicate

sections.title: Tier 1: Syndicate Enforcer
sections.content:
*The muscle of the Kaelen criminal empire, armed with cheap kinetic weapons and bad attitudes.*
*   **Type:** Minion
*   **Difficulty:** 10
*   **Attack Modifier:** +2
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 6, Severe 12
*   **Hit Points:** 3
*   **Stress:** 2
*   **Experience:** Intimidation (+2)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Scrap-Pistol* (Close range, 1d8+1 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Strength in Numbers.* When attacking a target that is engaged in Melee with another Syndicate ally, the Enforcer gains Advantage on the attack roll.
*   **GM Tactics:** Enforcers are cowards when alone but vicious in packs. They will always try to surround a single PC to trigger their passive feature.

sections.title: Tier 1: Syndicate Fixer
sections.content:
*The mid-level bosses who coordinate smuggling rings and extortion rackets.*
*   **Type:** Support
*   **Difficulty:** 12
*   **Attack Modifier:** +3
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 7, Severe 14
*   **Hit Points:** 4
*   **Stress:** 3
*   **Experience:** Street Smarts (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Concealed Blade* (Melee range, 1d6+2 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Mark the Target.* The Fixer spends 1 Stress to designate a PC. All Syndicate allies gain +2 to their attack rolls against that PC until the end of the round.
*   **GM Tactics:** Fixers stay behind cover, directing Enforcers to focus fire on the party's weakest member.

sections.title: Tier 2: Syndicate Breacher
sections.content:
*Heavily armored shock troops used for boarding actions and breaking stalemates.*
*   **Type:** Bruiser
*   **Difficulty:** 13
*   **Attack Modifier:** +5
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 12, Severe 20
*   **Hit Points:** 6
*   **Stress:** 4
*   **Experience:** Close Quarters Combat (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Heavy Shotgun* (Close range, 2d8+3 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Concussion Grenade.* The Breacher spends 2 Stress to throw a grenade. All PCs within Close range of the target point must make a Difficulty 13 Agility roll or take 2d6 physical damage and be Stunned until the end of their next turn.
*   **GM Tactics:** Breachers lead the charge, using grenades to disrupt enemy formations before closing in for devastating shotgun blasts.

sections.title: Tier 3: Syndicate "Cleaner"
sections.content:
*A high-end assassin sent to erase problems that the Fixers couldn't handle.*
*   **Type:** Skulk
*   **Difficulty:** 17
*   **Attack Modifier:** +8
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 12, Severe 22
*   **Hit Points:** 8
*   **Stress:** 6
*   **Experience:** Master of Disguise (+4)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Silenced Sniper Rifle* (Very Far range, 2d12+5 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Unseen.* The Cleaner cannot be targeted by attacks or spells if they are in cover or darkness, unless a PC first succeeds on a Difficulty 16 Instinct roll to locate them.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Vanish.* The Cleaner spends 1 Stress to immediately move up to Far range and enter stealth, ending any effects that require line of sight.
*   **GM Tactics:** Cleaners never fight fair. They shoot from maximum range, use *Vanish* to reposition, and target medics or squishy casters first.

sections.title: Tier 4: Syndicate Crime Lord (Boss)
sections.content:
*The untouchable head of a Kaelen cartel, fighting with expensive tech and disposable bodyguards.*
*   **Type:** Boss
*   **Difficulty:** 18
*   **Attack Modifier:** +8
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 20, Severe 35
*   **Hit Points:** 12
*   **Stress:** 8
*   **Experience:** Always Has an Escape Plan (+5)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Custom Hand-Cannon* (Far range, 3d10+6 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Meat Shields.* As long as the Crime Lord has at least one allied Minion or Standard adversary within Close range, the Crime Lord's Evasion increases by +2.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Call in the Boys.* The GM spends 2 Fear to summon 1d4+1 Syndicate Enforcers (Tier 1 Minions) into empty spaces within Far range.
*   **Feature (Reaction):** *Rigged to Blow.* When the Crime Lord is reduced to 0 HP, they can spend 1 Fear to detonate hidden explosives. All PCs within Close range take 4d8 physical damage, and the Crime Lord escapes in the chaos.
*   **GM Tactics:** The Crime Lord never fights fair. They surround themselves with Enforcers to boost their Evasion, shoot from a distance, and use *Rigged to Blow* to ensure they survive to fight another day.

---

chapters.content: The Kaelen Syndicate is a brutal, pragmatic organization born from centuries of Aurelian occupation. They fight with salvaged gear, overwhelming aggression, and a complete disregard for "honorable" combat. Their forces are a mix of desperate revolutionaries and hardened criminals.

chapters.title: 2. The Aurelian Empire

sections.title: Tier 1: Aurelian Legionnaire
sections.content:
*Highly disciplined, heavily armored shock troops of the Empire.*
*   **Type:** Standard
*   **Difficulty:** 13
*   **Attack Modifier:** +4
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 9, Severe 16
*   **Hit Points:** 5
*   **Stress:** 4
*   **Experience:** Military Tactics (+2)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Mag-Rifle* (Far range, 1d10+2 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Reaction):** *Phalanx Formation.* When an adjacent Aurelian ally is attacked, the Legionnaire can spend 1 Stress to force the attacker to roll with Disadvantage.
*   **GM Tactics:** Legionnaires fight in tight formations, using *Phalanx Formation* to protect each other while laying down disciplined volley fire.

sections.title: Tier 2: Aurelian Centurion
sections.content:
*Veteran squad leaders who inspire their troops and coordinate complex maneuvers.*
*   **Type:** Leader
*   **Difficulty:** 14
*   **Attack Modifier:** +5
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 11, Severe 19
*   **Hit Points:** 6
*   **Stress:** 5
*   **Experience:** Command Presence (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Plasma Pistol* (Far range, 1d8+3 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Hold the Line.* The Centurion spends 2 Stress. All Aurelian allies within Far range clear 1 Stress and gain +1 to their Evasion until the end of the round.
*   **GM Tactics:** Centurions prioritize keeping their Legionnaires alive and effective, using *Hold the Line* when the party starts dealing heavy damage.

sections.title: Tier 2: Imperial Infiltrator
sections.content:
*Aurelian stealth operatives used for political assassinations and sabotage.*
*   **Type:** Skulk
*   **Difficulty:** 15
*   **Attack Modifier:** +6
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 10, Severe 18
*   **Hit Points:** 6
*   **Stress:** 5
*   **Experience:** Shadows and Silence (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Poisoned Dagger* (Melee range, 1d6+2 physical damage plus 1d6 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Active Camouflage.* The Infiltrator is invisible while moving. A PC must succeed on a Difficulty 15 Instinct roll to target them with a ranged attack.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Throat Slit.* The Infiltrator spends 2 Stress to make a Standard Attack against a Vulnerable target. If it hits, the target takes an additional 2d6 physical damage and is Silenced until the end of their next turn.
*   **GM Tactics:** Infiltrators bypass the frontline entirely, using *Active Camouflage* to reach the party's casters or medics, then using *Throat Slit* to shut them down.

sections.title: Tier 3: Aurelian Praetorian Guard
sections.content:
*The Emperor's personal guard, clad in powered armor and wielding relic-tech weapons.*
*   **Type:** Solo
*   **Difficulty:** 16
*   **Attack Modifier:** +7
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 18, Severe 32
*   **Hit Points:** 12
*   **Stress:** 8
*   **Experience:** Relentless Pursuit (+4)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Plasma Halberd* (Melee range, 3d10+6 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Powered Armor.* The Praetorian reduces all incoming physical damage by 5 before comparing it to their thresholds.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Overwhelming Strike.* The Praetorian spends 2 Stress to make a Standard Attack. If it hits, the target is knocked prone and must mark 1 Stress.
*   **Feature (Reaction):** *Deflect.* When targeted by a ranged attack, the Praetorian can spend 1 Fear to roll a d20. On a 15 or higher, the attack is deflected and deals no damage.
*   **GM Tactics:** A Praetorian is a walking tank. They march straight into the center of the party, relying on their armor and *Deflect* to survive while crushing the biggest threat with their halberd.

sections.title: Tier 4: Imperial Dreadnought (Vehicle/Boss)
sections.content:
*A massive, heavily armed walker deployed only when a settlement needs to be erased.*
*   **Type:** Solo
*   **Difficulty:** 18
*   **Attack Modifier:** +9
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 25, Severe 45
*   **Hit Points:** 15
*   **Stress:** 10
*   **Experience:** Siege Warfare (+5)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Twin Rotary Cannons* (Far range, 4d8+8 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Heavy Plating.* The Dreadnought is immune to all physical damage from Tier 1 and Tier 2 weapons.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Orbital Bombardment.* The GM spends 3 Fear. The Dreadnought paints a target area. At the start of the GM's next turn, all creatures within Far range of that point take 5d10 magic damage.
*   **GM Tactics:** The Dreadnought is a slow, inevitable force of destruction. It uses *Orbital Bombardment* to force the party out of cover, then mows them down with its cannons.

---

chapters.content: The Aurelian Empire fights with the arrogance of a civilization that believes it is destined to rule. Their troops are highly disciplined, equipped with top-tier energy weapons, and supported by devastating artillery.

chapters.title: 3. The Mindclave

sections.title: Tier 1: Mindclave Hound
sections.content:
*Low-level telepaths (P1-P3) trained to hunt down rogues and dissenters.*
*   **Type:** Skulk
*   **Difficulty:** 11
*   **Attack Modifier:** +3
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 5, Severe 10
*   **Hit Points:** 3
*   **Stress:** 3
*   **Experience:** Tracking (+2)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Stun Baton* (Melee range, 1d6+1 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Surface Scan.* The Hound always knows the exact location of any PC within Close range, even if they are invisible or in stealth.
*   **GM Tactics:** Hounds use their *Surface Scan* to flush out hiding PCs, then close in to stun them for capture.

sections.title: Tier 2: Mindclave Inquisitor
sections.content:
*Telepathic interrogators who use psychic pressure to break their enemies.*
*   **Type:** Support
*   **Difficulty:** 14
*   **Attack Modifier:** +5
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 10, Severe 18
*   **Hit Points:** 6
*   **Stress:** 5
*   **Experience:** Psychological Warfare (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Neural Whip* (Close range, 1d8+3 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Mind Spike.* The Inquisitor spends 2 Stress to force a PC within Far range to make a Difficulty 14 Presence roll. On a failure, the PC marks 2 Stress and is Vulnerable until the end of their next turn.
*   **GM Tactics:** Inquisitors stay out of melee, using *Mind Spike* to soften up targets for their Hounds or allied mercenaries.

sections.title: Tier 3: Mindclave Handler
sections.content:
*High-level telepaths (P7-P9) who control squads of dominated thralls.*
*   **Type:** Leader
*   **Difficulty:** 16
*   **Attack Modifier:** +7
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 14, Severe 26
*   **Hit Points:** 9
*   **Stress:** 7
*   **Experience:** Puppet Master (+4)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Psychic Blast* (Far range, 2d10+4 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Command Thrall.* The Handler spends 1 Stress to command an allied Minion or Standard adversary to immediately make a Standard Attack as a Free Action.
*   **Feature (Reaction):** *Meat Shield.* When the Handler is attacked, they can spend 1 Fear to force an adjacent allied Minion to take the damage instead.
*   **GM Tactics:** Handlers surround themselves with dominated thugs (use Syndicate Enforcer stats), using them as disposable weapons and shields.

sections.title: Tier 3: Mindclave Eradicator
sections.content:
*A telekinetic specialist trained to crush armor and throw heavy objects.*
*   **Type:** Bruiser
*   **Difficulty:** 16
*   **Attack Modifier:** +7
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 16, Severe 30
*   **Hit Points:** 10
*   **Stress:** 6
*   **Experience:** Telekinetic Mastery (+4)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Kinetic Crush* (Close range, 3d8+4 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Kinetic Shield.* The Eradicator reduces all incoming physical damage by 3.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Telekinetic Throw.* The Eradicator spends 2 Stress to hurl a heavy object (or a PC) up to Far range. The target takes 2d10 physical damage and is knocked prone.
*   **GM Tactics:** Eradicators are the Mindclave's heavy hitters. They use *Telekinetic Throw* to disrupt the party's positioning and isolate targets for their Hounds to finish off.

sections.title: Tier 4: Mindclave High Councilor (Boss)
sections.content:
*The supreme leader of the telepathic faction, capable of dominating entire crowds.*
*   **Type:** Solo
*   **Difficulty:** 19
*   **Attack Modifier:** +9
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 18, Severe 32
*   **Hit Points:** 12
*   **Stress:** 12
*   **Experience:** Absolute Control (+5)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Psychic Crush* (Far range, 3d12+6 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Mental Fortress.* The High Councilor is immune to all magic damage and psychic effects.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Dominate.* The High Councilor spends 4 Stress to force a PC to make a Difficulty 19 Presence roll. On a failure, the GM controls that PC's next turn.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Psychic Scream.* The GM spends 3 Fear. All PCs within Very Far range take 4d8 magic damage and are Stunned until the end of their next turn.
*   **GM Tactics:** The High Councilor is a terrifying opponent who turns the party against itself. They use *Dominate* on the party's heaviest hitter, then unleash *Psychic Scream* when surrounded.

---

chapters.content: The Mindclave uses psychological warfare, telepathic domination, and specialized enforcement agents to maintain their monopoly on human telepaths. They rarely fight fair, preferring to break their enemies' minds before a single shot is fired.

chapters.title: 4. The Valari Collective

sections.title: Tier 1: Valari Initiate
sections.content:
*Young warriors of the Vael'Kari caste, eager to prove their worth in battle.*
*   **Type:** Standard
*   **Difficulty:** 12
*   **Attack Modifier:** +3
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 7, Severe 14
*   **Hit Points:** 4
*   **Stress:** 3
*   **Experience:** Martial Discipline (+2)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Crystal Blade* (Melee range, 1d8+2 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Flurry of Strikes.* The Initiate spends 1 Stress to make two Standard Attacks against the same target. If both hit, the target is pushed back to Close range.
*   **GM Tactics:** Initiates are aggressive and fast, using *Flurry of Strikes* to overwhelm opponents and control the battlefield's spacing.

sections.title: Tier 2: Valari Bio-Construct
sections.content:
*A hulking, genetically engineered beast used as a living siege engine.*
*   **Type:** Bruiser
*   **Difficulty:** 12
*   **Attack Modifier:** +6
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 14, Severe 24
*   **Hit Points:** 8
*   **Stress:** 3
*   **Experience:** Unstoppable Force (+2)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Bone-Scythe Arm* (Melee range, 2d8+4 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Regenerative Tissue.* At the start of the GM's turn, if the Bio-Construct has not taken magic damage since its last turn, it clears 1 HP.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Frenzied Charge.* The GM spends 1 Fear. The Bio-Construct moves up to Far range in a straight line and makes a Standard Attack against every PC in its path.
*   **GM Tactics:** The Bio-Construct is a blunt instrument. It charges through the party's lines, forcing them to scatter or be crushed.

sections.title: Tier 3: Valari Blademaster
sections.content:
*Elite warriors who have mastered the art of soul-crystal weaponry.*
*   **Type:** Solo
*   **Difficulty:** 16
*   **Attack Modifier:** +8
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 15, Severe 28
*   **Hit Points:** 10
*   **Stress:** 6
*   **Experience:** Perfect Form (+4)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Soul-Crystal Glaive* (Melee range, 3d8+5 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Evasion Mastery.* The Blademaster's Difficulty increases by +2 against all ranged attacks.
*   **Feature (Reaction):** *Riposte.* When a PC misses the Blademaster with a melee attack, the Blademaster can spend 1 Stress to immediately make a Standard Attack against them.
*   **GM Tactics:** The Blademaster is a nightmare in melee combat. They close the distance quickly, relying on *Evasion Mastery* to survive the approach, and punish any missed attacks with *Riposte*.

sections.title: Tier 4: Valari High Priest (Leader)
sections.content:
*A spiritual leader who channels the radiant energy of the Valari ancestors.*
*   **Type:** Leader
*   **Difficulty:** 19
*   **Attack Modifier:** +9
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 18, Severe 32
*   **Hit Points:** 12
*   **Stress:** 10
*   **Experience:** Voice of the Ancestors (+5)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Radiant Blast* (Far range, 3d10+5 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Ancestral Blessing.* The High Priest spends 2 Stress. All Valari allies within Far range clear 2 Stress and gain Advantage on their next attack roll.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Blinding Light.* The GM spends 2 Fear. All PCs within Far range must make a Difficulty 18 Agility roll or be Blinded until the end of their next turn.
*   **GM Tactics:** The High Priest stays behind their Blademasters and Bio-Constructs, using *Ancestral Blessing* to keep them fighting at peak efficiency, and *Blinding Light* to protect themselves if the party breaks through the line.

---

chapters.content: The Valari fight with elegant, devastating energy weapons and genetically engineered bio-constructs. Their warriors are fanatically devoted to their caste and their spiritual beliefs.

chapters.title: 5. The Eclipse-Touched (Cultists)

sections.title: Tier 1: Void-Spawn
sections.content:
*Mindless, shadowy creatures summoned from the dark spaces between stars.*
*   **Type:** Minion
*   **Difficulty:** 9
*   **Attack Modifier:** +2
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 5, Severe 10
*   **Hit Points:** 2
*   **Stress:** 0 (Immune to Stress)
*   **Experience:** Relentless Hunger (+1)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Shadow Claws* (Melee range, 1d6 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Swarm.* For every two Void-Spawn adjacent to a PC, that PC's Evasion is reduced by 1.
*   **GM Tactics:** Void-Spawn are cannon fodder. They swarm the party in large numbers, dragging down their Evasion so the cultists can land their spells.

sections.title: Tier 2: Void-Caller
sections.content:
*Fanatics who have stared too long into the deep space anomalies and brought something back.*
*   **Type:** Leader
*   **Difficulty:** 13
*   **Attack Modifier:** +4
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 8, Severe 15
*   **Hit Points:** 5
*   **Stress:** 6
*   **Experience:** Occult Rituals (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Shadow Bolt* (Far range, 1d10+3 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Summon the Dark.* The Void-Caller spends 2 Stress to summon 1d4 Void-Spawns (Tier 1 Minions) into empty spaces within Close range.
*   **Feature (Reaction):** *Martyrdom.* When the Void-Caller is reduced to 0 HP, the GM can spend 1 Fear to cause them to explode in a burst of dark energy. All PCs within Close range take 2d6 magic damage.
*   **GM Tactics:** Void-Callers stay in the backline, summoning Void-Spawn to tie up the party while they hurl Shadow Bolts. When cornered, they gladly embrace *Martyrdom*.

sections.title: Tier 3: Void-Channeler
sections.content:
*An Eclipse cultist who sacrifices their own life force to unleash devastating magic.*
*   **Type:** Artillery
*   **Difficulty:** 15
*   **Attack Modifier:** +8
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 12, Severe 22
*   **Hit Points:** 8
*   **Stress:** 8
*   **Experience:** Dark Rituals (+4)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Void Bolt* (Very Far range, 2d12+4 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Blood Sacrifice.* The Channeler can choose to take 1d6 direct HP damage (bypassing thresholds) to increase the damage of their next *Void Bolt* by 3d12.
*   **Feature (Reaction):** *Void Step.* When targeted by a melee attack, the Channeler can spend 1 Stress to teleport up to Far range before the attack resolves.
*   **GM Tactics:** Channelers are glass cannons. They stay at maximum range, using *Void Step* to escape melee, and spam *Blood Sacrifice* to deal massive damage before they are killed.

sections.title: Tier 4: The Void Avatar (Boss)
sections.content:
*A physical manifestation of the dark energy from beyond the galactic rim.*
*   **Type:** Solo
*   **Difficulty:** 18
*   **Attack Modifier:** +10
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 25, Severe 45
*   **Hit Points:** 15
*   **Stress:** 10
*   **Experience:** Cosmic Horror (+5)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Annihilation Wave* (Far range, 4d10+8 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Aura of Despair.* Any PC who starts their turn within Close range of the Avatar must mark 1 Stress.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Reality Tear.* The Avatar spends 3 Stress to tear a hole in space. All PCs within Far range must make a Difficulty 18 Agility roll. On a failure, they take 3d12 magic damage and are teleported to a random location within the room.
*   **Feature (Reaction):** *Consume Hope.* When a PC spends Hope within Far range, the GM can spend 2 Fear to cancel the effect of the Hope and force the PC to mark 1 Stress instead.
*   **GM Tactics:** The Void Avatar is a campaign-ending threat. It uses *Reality Tear* to disrupt the party's positioning and *Consume Hope* to drain their resources, all while its *Aura of Despair* slowly breaks their minds.

---

chapters.content: The Eclipse-Touched are fanatical servants of the ancient void-entities. They use dark magic, sacrificial rituals, and summoned horrors to achieve their inscrutable goals.

chapters.title: 6. The Frontier and Progenitor Relics

sections.title: Tier 1: Progenitor Swarm-Drone
sections.content:
*Tiny, self-replicating repair drones that overwhelm intruders in ancient ruins.*
*   **Type:** Minion
*   **Difficulty:** 10
*   **Attack Modifier:** +2
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 4, Severe 8
*   **Hit Points:** 2
*   **Stress:** 0 (Immune to Stress)
*   **Experience:** Hive Mind (+1)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Cutting Laser* (Close range, 1d4 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Self-Replication.* At the start of the GM's turn, if there are at least two Swarm-Drones adjacent to each other, they spawn one new Swarm-Drone in an empty adjacent space.
*   **GM Tactics:** Swarm-Drones are weak individually but multiply quickly. The GM should group them together to trigger *Self-Replication*, forcing the party to use area-of-effect attacks to clear them out.

sections.title: Tier 1: Station Security Drone
sections.content:
*Floating, spherical automatons programmed to pacify riots and eliminate intruders.*
*   **Type:** Ranged
*   **Difficulty:** 11
*   **Attack Modifier:** +3
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 5, Severe 10
*   **Hit Points:** 3
*   **Stress:** 0 (Immune to Stress)
*   **Experience:** Targeting Algorithms (+2)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Stun Laser* (Close range, 1d6 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Pacification Protocol.* The drone spends 1 Fear to overcharge its laser. If the attack hits, the target must mark 1 Stress in addition to taking damage.
*   **GM Tactics:** Drones operate in squads, hovering out of melee range and using *Pacification Protocol* to subdue targets non-lethally.

sections.title: Tier 2: Void-Stalker (Wildlife)
sections.content:
*A predatory, silicon-based lifeform that hunts in the asteroid fields and derelict ships.*
*   **Type:** Skulk
*   **Difficulty:** 13
*   **Attack Modifier:** +5
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 10, Severe 18
*   **Hit Points:** 6
*   **Stress:** 4
*   **Experience:** Apex Predator (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Monofilament Claws* (Melee range, 2d6+2 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Vacuum Adaptation.* The Void-Stalker ignores all penalties for fighting in zero-gravity or vacuum environments.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Pounce.* The Void-Stalker spends 1 Stress to leap up to Far range and make a Standard Attack. If it hits, the target is knocked prone.
*   **GM Tactics:** Void-Stalkers are ambush predators. They wait in the dark, use *Pounce* to isolate a single PC, and drag them away from the group.

sections.title: Tier 3: Progenitor Sentinel
sections.content:
*An ancient, crystalline construct awakened from a deep slumber to protect a ruin.*
*   **Type:** Bruiser
*   **Difficulty:** 15
*   **Attack Modifier:** +6
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 20, Severe 35
*   **Hit Points:** 10
*   **Stress:** 0 (Immune to Stress)
*   **Experience:** Ancient Geometry (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Hard-Light Beam* (Far range, 3d8+4 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Crystalline Matrix.* The Sentinel is immune to all conditions (Blinded, Restrained, Vulnerable, etc.).
*   **Feature (Action):** *Gravity Well.* The GM spends 2 Fear. All PCs within Far range must make a Difficulty 15 Strength roll. On a failure, they are pulled into Melee range of the Sentinel and take 2d6 physical damage.
*   **GM Tactics:** Sentinels are implacable guardians. They use *Gravity Well* to pull ranged attackers into melee, then crush them with their massive bulk.

sections.title: Tier 4: Progenitor Leviathan (Boss/Vehicle)
sections.content:
*A massive, city-sized construct that patrols the deep frontier, destroying anything that approaches its designated sector.*
*   **Type:** Solo
*   **Difficulty:** 17
*   **Attack Modifier:** +8
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 22, Severe 40
*   **Hit Points:** 14
*   **Stress:** 0 (Immune to Stress)
*   **Experience:** Extinction Protocol (+5)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Antimatter Lance* (Very Far range, 4d10+6 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Energy Shielding.* The Leviathan reduces all incoming magic damage by 5 before comparing it to its thresholds.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Launch Drones.* The GM spends 2 Fear to deploy 1d4 Station Security Drones (Tier 1 Ranged) into empty spaces within Far range.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Core Overload.* The Leviathan spends 3 Fear to unleash a wave of destructive energy. All PCs within Very Far range must make a Difficulty 17 Agility roll. On a failure, they take 4d12 magic damage and their ship/vehicle loses 1d4 Armor Slots.
*   **GM Tactics:** The Leviathan is a massive set-piece encounter. It uses *Launch Drones* to distract the party while charging its *Core Overload* for a devastating area-of-effect attack.

---

chapters.content: The space surrounding Nexus 9 is largely uncharted, filled with the ruins of ancient civilizations, hostile wildlife, and automated defenses left behind by the Progenitors.

chapters.title: 7. The Terran Coalition

sections.title: Tier 1: Terran Marine
sections.content:
*Standard infantry equipped with reliable kinetic weapons and heavy body armor.*
*   **Type:** Standard
*   **Difficulty:** 12
*   **Attack Modifier:** +3
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 8, Severe 15
*   **Hit Points:** 5
*   **Stress:** 3
*   **Experience:** Breach and Clear (+2)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Assault Rifle* (Far range, 1d8+2 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Frag Grenade.* The Marine spends 1 Stress to throw a grenade. All PCs within Close range of the target point must make a Difficulty 12 Agility roll or take 2d6 physical damage.
*   **GM Tactics:** Marines use cover effectively, lay down suppressing fire, and use grenades to flush PCs out of entrenched positions.

sections.title: Tier 1: Terran Combat Medic
sections.content:
*Field medics who keep the Marines fighting long past their breaking point.*
*   **Type:** Support
*   **Difficulty:** 11
*   **Attack Modifier:** +2
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 6, Severe 12
*   **Hit Points:** 4
*   **Stress:** 4
*   **Experience:** Triage (+2)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Service Pistol* (Close range, 1d6+1 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Combat Stim.* The Medic spends 1 Stress to heal an adjacent ally for 1d6 HP and clear 1 Stress.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Smoke Grenade.* The Medic spends 1 Stress to create a cloud of smoke (Close range radius). All ranged attacks into or out of the smoke suffer Disadvantage.
*   **GM Tactics:** Medics stay behind the Marines, using *Smoke Grenade* to cover advances or retreats, and *Combat Stim* to keep the heavy hitters alive.

sections.title: Tier 2: Terran Heavy Gunner
sections.content:
*Support troops carrying massive rotary cannons that tear through armor and cover.*
*   **Type:** Artillery
*   **Difficulty:** 13
*   **Attack Modifier:** +5
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 12, Severe 20
*   **Hit Points:** 6
*   **Stress:** 5
*   **Experience:** Suppressing Fire (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Rotary Cannon* (Far range, 2d8+3 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Pin Down.* The Gunner spends 2 Stress to target a specific PC. That PC must make a Difficulty 14 Agility roll. On a failure, their movement is reduced to 0 until the end of their next turn, and they take 1d8 physical damage.
*   **GM Tactics:** Gunners find a good vantage point and use *Pin Down* on the party's most mobile characters, allowing the Marines to close in for the kill.

sections.title: Tier 3: Terran "Goliath" Mech
sections.content:
*A piloted, bipedal walker deployed for anti-armor and siege operations.*
*   **Type:** Bruiser
*   **Difficulty:** 16
*   **Attack Modifier:** +7
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 18, Severe 32
*   **Hit Points:** 10
*   **Stress:** 6
*   **Experience:** Heavy Armor (+4)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Anti-Materiel Rockets* (Very Far range, 3d10+5 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Reinforced Chassis.* The Goliath reduces all incoming physical damage by 4.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Stomp.* The Goliath spends 2 Stress to make a melee attack against an adjacent PC (3d8+5 physical damage). If it hits, the target is knocked prone and must mark 1 Stress.
*   **GM Tactics:** The Goliath is a walking tank. It uses its rockets at range, then wades into melee to *Stomp* anyone foolish enough to get close.

---

chapters.content: The military arm of Earth and its colonies. They rely on overwhelming firepower, tactical coordination, and heavy mechanization. They are not subtle, but they are incredibly effective in a stand-up fight.

chapters.title: 8. The Technoseers

sections.title: Tier 1: Technoseer Acolyte
sections.content:
*Low-level initiates who interface directly with station systems and enemy tech.*
*   **Type:** Support
*   **Difficulty:** 12
*   **Attack Modifier:** +3
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 6, Severe 12
*   **Hit Points:** 4
*   **Stress:** 4
*   **Experience:** System Override (+2)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Arc-Welder* (Close range, 1d6+2 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Weapon Hack.* The Acolyte spends 1 Stress to target a PC's weapon. The PC must make a Difficulty 12 Knowledge roll. On a failure, their weapon misfires, dealing 1d4 damage to the PC and becoming unusable until the end of their next turn.
*   **GM Tactics:** Acolytes avoid direct combat, using *Weapon Hack* to disable the party's heaviest hitters while their allies attack.

sections.title: Tier 2: Umbral Covenant Heretic
sections.content:
*A rogue Technoseer who uses forbidden stealth tech to steal secrets and assassinate rivals.*
*   **Type:** Skulk
*   **Difficulty:** 15
*   **Attack Modifier:** +6
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 10, Severe 18
*   **Hit Points:** 6
*   **Stress:** 5
*   **Experience:** Ghost in the Machine (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Phase Blade* (Melee range, 2d6+3 magic damage; ignores physical armor)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Optical Camouflage.* The Heretic is invisible while standing still. A PC must succeed on a Difficulty 15 Instinct roll to spot them before they attack.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Data Spike.* The Heretic spends 2 Stress to make a Standard Attack. If it hits, the target takes normal damage and the Heretic steals 1 Hope from the party's pool.
*   **GM Tactics:** Heretics wait in ambush, using *Optical Camouflage* to get close, then striking with their *Phase Blade* and stealing Hope to fuel the GM's Fear pool.

sections.title: Tier 3: Technoseer Magus
sections.content:
*High-ranking priests of the machine god, capable of manipulating energy fields.*
*   **Type:** Leader
*   **Difficulty:** 16
*   **Attack Modifier:** +7
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 14, Severe 26
*   **Hit Points:** 8
*   **Stress:** 7
*   **Experience:** Master of the Code (+4)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Plasma Emitter* (Far range, 3d8+4 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Deploy Forcefield.* The Magus spends 2 Stress to create a shimmering barrier (Close range radius). Allies inside the barrier gain +2 Evasion against ranged attacks.
*   **Feature (Action):** *EMP Burst.* The GM spends 2 Fear. All PCs within Far range must make a Difficulty 16 Agility roll or take 2d10 magic damage and lose the use of any tech-based items or armor features until the end of their next turn.
*   **GM Tactics:** The Magus controls the battlefield, using *Deploy Forcefield* to protect their Acolytes and *EMP Burst* to strip the party of their technological advantages.

---

chapters.content: A secretive, monastic order that worships logic and hoards ancient technology. They replace their flesh with cybernetics and view the rest of the galaxy as dangerously irrational.

chapters.title: 9. Independents and Station Security

sections.title: Tier 1: Nexus 9 Security Officer
sections.content:
*The overworked, underpaid cops who try to keep the peace on the station.*
*   **Type:** Standard
*   **Difficulty:** 11
*   **Attack Modifier:** +2
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 7, Severe 14
*   **Hit Points:** 4
*   **Stress:** 3
*   **Experience:** Crowd Control (+2)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Stun Baton* (Melee range, 1d6+1 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Reaction):** *Shield Wall.* When an adjacent ally is attacked, the Officer can spend 1 Stress to grant them +1 Evasion against that attack.
*   **GM Tactics:** Security Officers fight defensively, using *Shield Wall* to protect each other and relying on their stun batons to subdue rather than kill.

sections.title: Tier 1: Pirate Boarding Party
sections.content:
*Desperate raiders who attack civilian ships and frontier outposts.*
*   **Type:** Minion
*   **Difficulty:** 10
*   **Attack Modifier:** +2
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 6, Severe 12
*   **Hit Points:** 3
*   **Stress:** 2
*   **Experience:** Boarding Action (+2)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Boarding Axe* (Melee range, 1d8+1 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Bloodlust.* When a Pirate attacks a PC who is currently below their Major Damage Threshold, the Pirate deals +2 damage.
*   **GM Tactics:** Pirates are aggressive and reckless. They swarm the weakest-looking party member, trying to trigger their *Bloodlust* feature as quickly as possible.

sections.title: Tier 2: Freelance Bounty Hunter
sections.content:
*A heavily armed professional who tracks targets across the galaxy for credits.*
*   **Type:** Skulk
*   **Difficulty:** 14
*   **Attack Modifier:** +5
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 12, Severe 20
*   **Hit Points:** 6
*   **Stress:** 5
*   **Experience:** I Always Get My Mark (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Sniper Rifle* (Very Far range, 2d10+3 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Tracking Dart.* The Hunter spends 1 Stress to fire a dart at a PC. For the rest of the scene, the Hunter has Advantage on all attacks against that PC, and the PC cannot hide from them.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Jetpack Escape.* The Hunter spends 1 Stress to immediately move up to Far range, ignoring difficult terrain and obstacles.
*   **GM Tactics:** Bounty Hunters are pragmatic. They use *Tracking Dart* on their primary target, shoot from maximum range, and use *Jetpack Escape* if the party gets too close.

---

chapters.content: The people who keep Nexus 9 running, and the scum who prey on its trade routes. These adversaries can be encountered anywhere, working for anyone who pays.

chapters.title: 10. The Eclipse-Corrupted

sections.title: Tier 2: Corrupted Diplomat
sections.content:
*A once-respected envoy whose mind has been shattered by the Eclipse, now spreading paranoia and violence.*
*   **Type:** Support
*   **Difficulty:** 14
*   **Attack Modifier:** +5
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 10, Severe 18
*   **Hit Points:** 6
*   **Stress:** 5
*   **Experience:** Twisted Words (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Psychic Dissonance* (Far range, 2d8+3 magic damage)
