*   **Feature (Action):** *Sow Discord.* The Diplomat spends 2 Stress to target a PC. That PC must make a Difficulty 14 Presence roll. On a failure, they must immediately make a Standard Attack against an ally of the GM's choice as a Free Action.
*   **GM Tactics:** The Diplomat avoids direct combat, using *Sow Discord* to turn the party's heaviest hitters against their own medics and casters.

sections.title: Tier 2: Corrupted Marine
sections.content:
*A Terran soldier whose armor has fused to their mutated flesh, driven by an endless, silent rage.*
*   **Type:** Bruiser
*   **Difficulty:** 13
*   **Attack Modifier:** +6
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 14, Severe 24
*   **Hit Points:** 8
*   **Stress:** 4
*   **Experience:** Unfeeling Flesh (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Fused Chain-Blade* (Melee range, 2d10+4 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Void-Hardened.* The Marine is immune to the Stunned and Vulnerable conditions.
*   **Feature (Reaction):** *Spiteful Blood.* When the Marine takes physical damage from a melee attack, the attacker takes 1d6 magic damage from acidic, void-tainted blood.
*   **GM Tactics:** The Marine is a relentless juggernaut. They charge the nearest PC and stay in melee, punishing anyone who tries to fight them hand-to-hand with *Spiteful Blood*.

sections.title: Tier 3: Corrupted Valari
sections.content:
*A fallen warrior whose soul-crystal has turned black, draining Hope from the world around them.*
*   **Type:** Solo
*   **Difficulty:** 17
*   **Attack Modifier:** +8
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 18, Severe 32
*   **Hit Points:** 12
*   **Stress:** 8
*   **Experience:** Shattered Honor (+4)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Dark-Light Glaive* (Melee range, 3d10+5 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Aura of Despair.* Any PC who starts their turn within Close range of the Corrupted Valari must mark 1 Stress.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Drain Hope.* The GM spends 2 Fear. The Valari targets a PC within Far range. That PC loses 1 Hope, and the Valari heals 2d6 HP.
*   **GM Tactics:** The Corrupted Valari is a terrifying solo threat. They use their *Aura of Despair* to slowly break the party's minds, and *Drain Hope* to sustain themselves through a long fight.

---

chapters.content: When the Eclipse touches a mind, it twists the host's original abilities into something dark. These adversaries are tragic figures — former allies or neutral parties who have been hollowed out and filled with void energy.

chapters.title: 11. Deep Space Horrors

sections.title: Tier 2: Hyperspace Phantom
sections.content:
*A creature that phases in and out of reality, attacking from the blind spots of a ship's sensors.*
*   **Type:** Skulk
*   **Difficulty:** 15
*   **Attack Modifier:** +6
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 8, Severe 15
*   **Hit Points:** 5
*   **Stress:** 6
*   **Experience:** Out of Phase (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Dimensional Tear* (Close range, 2d8+4 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Incorporeal.* The Phantom takes half damage from all physical attacks.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Phase Shift.* The Phantom spends 1 Stress to teleport up to Far range, passing through solid objects and bulkheads.
*   **GM Tactics:** Phantoms are ambush predators. They use *Phase Shift* to bypass armor and cover, strike with *Dimensional Tear*, and vanish before the party can retaliate.

sections.title: Tier 3: Silicate Swarm (Hazard)
sections.content:
*A living asteroid field that consumes starship hulls and organic matter with equal hunger.*
*   **Type:** Hazard / Swarm
*   **Difficulty:** 14
*   **Attack Modifier:** +7
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 20, Severe 35
*   **Hit Points:** 10
*   **Stress:** 0 (Immune to Stress)
*   **Experience:** Devouring Cloud (+4)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Abrasive Cloud* (Close range radius, 3d6+5 physical damage to all targets in area)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Amorphous.* The Swarm is immune to all single-target attacks that do not deal area-of-effect damage.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Consume.* The GM spends 2 Fear. The Swarm targets a vehicle or heavily armored PC. The target's Armor value is permanently reduced by 1.
*   **GM Tactics:** The Swarm is an environmental puzzle as much as a combat encounter. The party must use explosives, environmental hazards, or ship weapons to destroy it before it *Consumes* their defenses.

sections.title: Tier 4: The Whisperer in the Dark (Boss)
sections.content:
*An ancient entity that traps the party in a shared psychic nightmare, feeding on their deepest fears.*
*   **Type:** Boss
*   **Difficulty:** 20
*   **Attack Modifier:** +10
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Major 25, Severe 45
*   **Hit Points:** 15
*   **Stress:** 12
*   **Experience:** Architect of Nightmares (+5)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Mind Flay* (Very Far range, 4d10+8 magic damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Illusory Terrain.* The battlefield constantly shifts. At the start of each round, the GM can rearrange all cover, hazards, and difficult terrain.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Manifest Fear.* The Whisperer spends 3 Stress to summon a phantasmal copy of a PC's greatest fear (use Tier 2 Bruiser stats). The phantasm only deals damage to that specific PC.
*   **Feature (Reaction):** *Shatter Reality.* When the Whisperer takes Severe damage, the GM can spend 3 Fear to force all PCs to make a Difficulty 18 Instinct roll. On a failure, they take 3d12 magic damage and are Stunned.
*   **GM Tactics:** The Whisperer is a psychological boss fight. It uses *Illusory Terrain* to keep the party disoriented, *Manifest Fear* to isolate individuals, and *Shatter Reality* as a devastating counter-attack.

---

chapters.content: Threats found in the uncharted sectors of the Rimward Marches, where the laws of physics begin to break down.

chapters.title: 12. Political Threats (Non-Combat / Social)

sections.title: The Corrupt Senator
sections.content:
*A high-ranking official who uses bureaucratic red tape and legal threats to block the party.*
*   **Difficulty:** 16 (for Persuasion/Intimidation rolls)
*   **Hit Points:** 3
*   **Stress:** 6
*   **Experience:** Untouchable (+4)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Web of Influence.* The Senator cannot be harmed physically without the party immediately becoming Wanted by station security.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Red Tape.* The Senator spends 2 Stress to impose Disadvantage on the party's next social or investigation roll.

sections.title: The Fanatical Preacher
sections.content:
*A charismatic demagogue who incites riots and turns the civilian population against the crew.*
*   **Difficulty:** 14 (for Presence/Deception rolls)
*   **Hit Points:** 4
*   **Stress:** 5
*   **Experience:** Voice of the Masses (+3)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Zealous Following.* The Preacher is always surrounded by a crowd of devoted followers, making it impossible to speak to them privately.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Incite the Mob.* The Preacher spends 2 Stress to turn the crowd hostile. The party must immediately flee or face a Tier 2 Swarm encounter.

sections.title: The Diplomat
sections.content:
*A representative of one of the major factions, skilled in negotiation and deception.*
*   **Difficulty:** 14 (for Persuasion/Deception rolls)
*   **Hit Points:** 3
*   **Stress:** 4
*   **Experience:** Silver Tongue (+3)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Diplomatic Immunity.* The Diplomat cannot be attacked or detained without severe political consequences for the offending party.
*   **Feature (Reaction):** *Call Security.* When threatened, the Diplomat spends 1 Stress to summon 1d4 Station Security Drones (Tier 1 Ranged) to their location.

sections.title: The Information Broker
sections.content:
*A shadowy figure who deals in secrets, rumors, and blackmail.*
*   **Difficulty:** 15 (for Investigation/Intimidation rolls)
*   **Hit Points:** 4
*   **Stress:** 5
*   **Experience:** A Guy Who Knows A Guy (+4)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Eyes Everywhere.* The Broker always knows when they are being followed or surveilled.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Blackmail.* The Broker spends 2 Stress to reveal a damaging secret about a PC, forcing them to make a Difficulty 15 Presence roll. On a failure, the PC must comply with a reasonable request or mark 2 Stress.

sections.title: The Smuggler
sections.content:
*A rogue pilot who moves illicit goods past station security and faction blockades.*
*   **Difficulty:** 13 (for Piloting/Deception rolls)
*   **Hit Points:** 5
*   **Stress:** 4
*   **Experience:** Evasive Maneuvers (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Hold-Out Blaster* (Close range, 1d6+2 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Hidden Compartments.* The Smuggler always has a concealed weapon or escape route.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Distraction.* The Smuggler spends 1 Stress to create a diversion. All PCs must make a Difficulty 13 Instinct roll or lose their next action.

---

chapters.content: Not every threat on Nexus 9 can be solved with a weapon. These adversaries fight with words, influence, and the weight of bureaucracy. They are used in social encounters and investigation scenes.

chapters.title: 13. Encounter Building and GM Guidance

sections.title: Encounter Budgets (Fear Costs)
sections.content:
*   **Minor Encounter (0-2 Fear):** A brief skirmish with a few Minions or a single Standard adversary. Easily resolved without expending major resources.
*   **Standard Encounter (2-4 Fear):** A challenging fight against a mix of Standard, Support, and Bruiser adversaries. Requires tactical coordination and some resource expenditure.
*   **Major Encounter (4-8 Fear):** A significant battle against elite troops, a Leader, or a Solo adversary. High risk of PC death or severe consequences.
*   **Climactic Encounter (6-12 Fear):** A campaign-defining confrontation against a Boss or a massive vehicle. Requires the party to use all their abilities, Hope, and tactical ingenuity to survive.

sections.title: Environmental Hazards
sections.content:
The environment of Nexus 9 and the frontier is often as dangerous as the adversaries themselves. The GM can spend Fear to trigger these hazards during combat:
*   **Zero-Gravity (1 Fear):** All physical attacks made by PCs suffer a -1 penalty to the attack roll, and movement is halved unless the PC has Mag-Boots or a similar ability.
*   **Hull Breach (2 Fear):** A section of the room depressurizes. All PCs within Close range of the breach must make a Difficulty 14 Strength roll or be pulled toward the breach and take 2d6 physical damage from flying debris.
*   **Radiation Leak (1 Fear):** A damaged reactor or weapon vents radiation. All PCs in the affected area must mark 1 Stress at the start of their turn.

---

chapters.content: When building encounters in *Nexus 9*, the GM should consider the faction dynamics, the environment, and the Fear economy.

chapters.title: 14. Threat Clocks and Escalation Tracks

sections.title: The Aurelian Empire: "Annexation of the Frontier"
sections.content:
*The Empire seeks to bring the independent worlds surrounding Nexus 9 under their direct control.*
*   **Clock Size:** 8 segments
*   **Trigger:** The crew fails a mission that would have hindered Imperial logistics, or they publicly embarrass an Imperial official.
*   **Segment 2:** Imperial patrols increase in the sector. Smuggling becomes harder (Difficulty +1 to all Broker rolls).
*   **Segment 4:** The Empire establishes a blockade around a key independent world. Prices for goods on Nexus 9 double.
*   **Segment 6:** Imperial warships arrive at Nexus 9, demanding the station's surrender. The crew must navigate a tense diplomatic standoff.
*   **Segment 8 (Resolution):** The Empire annexes the sector by force. Nexus 9 becomes an occupied military outpost. The crew is declared enemies of the state.

sections.title: The Eclipse-Touched: "The Awakening"
sections.content:
*The cultists are attempting to summon a Void Avatar to consume the station.*
*   **Clock Size:** 6 segments
*   **Trigger:** The crew ignores rumors of missing persons in the lower decks, or they fail to stop a cult ritual.
*   **Segment 2:** Strange anomalies begin appearing on the station (flickering lights, sudden drops in temperature).
*   **Segment 4:** The cultists launch a coordinated attack on the station's security forces, plunging the lower decks into chaos.
*   **Segment 6 (Resolution):** The Void Avatar is summoned. The crew must face a Tier 4 Boss encounter immediately, or the station is destroyed.

sections.title: The Kaelen Syndicate: "The Underworld War"
sections.content:
*The Syndicate attempts to violently seize control of the station's black market from rival gangs.*
*   **Clock Size:** 4 segments
*   **Trigger:** The crew interferes with a Syndicate smuggling operation or kills a high-ranking Fixer.
*   **Segment 1:** Syndicate thugs begin openly extorting merchants in the Promenade.
*   **Segment 2:** A rival gang leader is assassinated in broad daylight. Station security is overwhelmed.
*   **Segment 3:** The Syndicate locks down the Underbelly, cutting off access to illegal goods and information brokers.
*   **Segment 4 (Resolution):** The Syndicate takes full control of the black market. All illicit purchases cost double, and the crew is marked for death by Syndicate assassins.

---

chapters.content: In *Nexus 9*, the galaxy does not wait for the players. Factions advance their agendas off-screen, and the GM tracks this progress using **Threat Clocks**. These are standard Daggerheart countdowns (usually 4, 6, or 8 segments) that advance when the players fail an action roll with Fear, or when they choose to ignore a faction's activities for too long.

chapters.title: 15. Encounter Seeds

sections.title: Diplomacy and Intrigue (Social Encounters)
sections.content:
*   **The Defector:** A high-ranking Mindclave telepath wants to defect to the Aurelian Empire and seeks the crew's help to smuggle them off the station. Both factions are hunting them.
*   **The Trade Dispute:** Two rival merchant guilds are on the verge of a violent turf war in the station's promenade. The crew must mediate a truce before station security intervenes with lethal force.
*   **The Blackmail:** A Syndicate Fixer has obtained compromising information about a crew member's past and demands they sabotage a rival faction's ship in exchange for silence.

sections.title: Sabotage and Espionage (Skulk Encounters)
sections.content:
*   **The Data Heist:** The crew is hired to infiltrate a secure Aurelian data vault and extract the blueprints for a new weapon system. They must bypass automated defenses and avoid detection by Praetorian Guards.
*   **The Sabotaged Reactor:** Someone has planted an explosive device on the station's main reactor. The crew must find and disarm it before it detonates, while dealing with the Syndicate thugs hired to protect it.
*   **The Inside Job:** A mole within the station's administration is leaking sensitive information to the Eclipse-Touched. The crew must identify the traitor without alerting them.

sections.title: War and Survival (Combat Encounters)
sections.content:
*   **The Boarding Party:** While traveling through the frontier, the crew's ship is ambushed and boarded by a Kaelen Syndicate raiding party. They must repel the boarders and repair their ship's engines before they are destroyed.
*   **The Progenitor Ruin:** The crew explores an ancient alien ruin on a desolate moon, only to awaken its crystalline defenders. They must survive waves of Progenitor Sentinels while trying to extract a valuable artifact.
*   **The Station Siege:** The Aurelian Empire launches a full-scale assault on Nexus 9. The crew must hold the line alongside station security against waves of Legionnaires and heavy armor.

sections.title: The Terran Coalition (Military Encounters)
sections.content:
*   **The Stranded Patrol:** A squad of Terran Marines and a Combat Medic are pinned down by Kaelen Syndicate forces in the lower decks. The crew must decide whether to intervene and save the Terrans, earning their favor but angering the Syndicate, or leave them to their fate.
*   **The Rogue Mech:** A Terran "Goliath" Mech pilot has suffered a psychological break and is rampaging through the docking bay. The crew must disable the mech without killing the pilot, while avoiding the Heavy Gunner trying to provide covering fire for their squadmate.
*   **The Checkpoint:** The Terran Coalition has set up an illegal blockade on a major trade route. The crew must negotiate passage with the commanding officer or fight their way through a squad of Marines and a Heavy Gunner.

sections.title: The Technoseers (Tech & Puzzle Encounters)
sections.content:
*   **The Stolen Relic:** A Technoseer Acolyte has stolen a dangerous Progenitor artifact from the station's museum. The crew must track them down and recover the artifact before the Acolyte accidentally triggers a station-wide EMP.
*   **The Heretic's Lair:** An Umbral Covenant Heretic is using a hidden server farm to siphon credits from the station's accounts. The crew must infiltrate the lair, bypass the Heretic's optical camouflage, and shut down the servers.
*   **The Logic Puzzle:** A Technoseer Magus challenges the crew to a battle of wits to prove their worthiness. The crew must solve a series of complex logic puzzles while defending themselves from the Magus's deployed forcefields and Acolyte assistants.

sections.title: Independents and Station Security (Urban Encounters)
sections.content:
*   **The Riot:** A protest in the Promenade turns violent. The crew must help Nexus 9 Security Officers pacify the crowd without using lethal force, while dealing with opportunistic Pirate Boarding Parties looting the shops.
*   **The Bounty:** A Freelance Bounty Hunter has cornered their mark—a sympathetic NPC the crew has worked with before—in a crowded cantina. The crew must defuse the situation or fight the Hunter to protect their friend.
*   **The Smuggler's Run:** A Smuggler offers the crew a lucrative but highly illegal job. If they accept, they must evade Station Security Drones and navigate a treacherous asteroid field to deliver the goods.

sections.title: The Eclipse-Corrupted (Tragic Encounters)
sections.content:
*   **The Fallen Friend:** A previously friendly NPC (use Corrupted Diplomat stats) has been infected by the Eclipse and is now spreading paranoia among the station's leadership. The crew must subdue them and find a way to cure the corruption.
*   **The Mutated Squad:** A squad of Terran Marines sent to investigate an anomaly has returned as Corrupted Marines. The crew must stop their rampage through the medical bay before they infect the patients.
*   **The Shattered Crystal:** A Corrupted Valari is draining the life force from the station's hydroponics bay. The crew must confront the fallen warrior and shatter their corrupted soul-crystal to stop the decay.

sections.title: Deep Space Horrors (Exploration Encounters)
sections.content:
*   **The Ghost Ship:** The crew answers a distress beacon from a derelict freighter, only to find it infested with Hyperspace Phantoms. They must repair the ship's engines and escape before the Phantoms drag them into the void.
*   **The Living Asteroid:** While navigating a dense asteroid field, the crew's ship is attacked by a Silicate Swarm. They must use their ship's weapons and evasive maneuvers to destroy the swarm before it consumes their hull.
*   **The Nightmare Realm:** The crew's ship is pulled into a psychic anomaly created by The Whisperer in the Dark. They must navigate a shifting, illusory landscape and confront manifestations of their own fears to escape.

sections.title: Political Threats (Social Encounters)
sections.content:
*   **The Kangaroo Court:** A Corrupt Senator has framed the crew for a crime they didn't commit. The crew must gather evidence to clear their names and expose the Senator's corruption during a highly publicized trial.
*   **The Demagogue:** A Fanatical Preacher is inciting a mob to attack the Valari embassy. The crew must use diplomacy and intimidation to disperse the crowd and discredit the Preacher before violence erupts.
*   **The Blackmail Ring:** An Information Broker has obtained sensitive data that could start a war between the Aurelian Empire and the Terran Coalition. The crew must infiltrate the Broker's network and delete the data before it can be sold.

---

*End of Adversaries & Threats Guide v3.1 — 52 Stat Blocks, 27 Encounter Seeds*

chapters.content: These seeds provide the GM with quick, thematic encounters that can be dropped into any session, categorized by the type of challenge they present.

chapters.title: The Gathering Storm — Overview
chapters.content:
**Version:** 1.0
**Date:** April 4, 2026
**Status:** Ready for Playtest

---

chapters.title: Introduction to the Campaign
chapters.content:
*The Gathering Storm* is a 12-chapter campaign designed to take a crew of characters from Level 1 to Level 10. It is divided into three major arcs. The campaign begins with local station politics and smuggling disputes, escalates into a system-wide war between major factions, and concludes with a desperate, galaxy-spanning battle against an ancient, cosmic threat.

**Running This Campaign:**
*   **Read-Aloud Text:** Text in blockquotes should be read aloud or paraphrased to the players to set the scene.
*   **Adversary Stats:** All adversaries referenced in bold (e.g., **Syndicate Enforcer**) can be found in the *Adversaries & Threats Guide*.
*   **Pacing:** Each chapter is designed to take 1-2 sessions of play (3-6 hours total).

---

chapters.title: ARC 1: THE FRAGILE PEACE (Levels 1-3)

sections.title: Chapter 1: The Midnight Cargo
sections.content:
**Level:** 1
**Featured Factions:** Terran Coalition, Kaelen Syndicate, The Underbelly

**The Hook:**
A routine customs inspection in the Red Ring turns deadly when a Kaelen freighter is found carrying a dormant Eclipse artifact. The crew is hired by Commander Vance to investigate the artifact's origin before the Kaelen Syndicate claims it as a weapon of war.

> *"The Red Ring smells of ozone, gun oil, and cheap antiseptic. Docking Bay Alpha is currently locked down behind a wall of Terran Coalition riot shields. Beyond the barricade, a Kaelen freighter sits dark and silent. Commander Vance stands at the edge of the cordon, rubbing his temples. 'I need people who don't wear a uniform,' he says, looking at your crew. 'There's something on that ship that killed my customs team without firing a shot. Find out what it is, and keep it out of Syndicate hands.'"*

**Encounter 1: The Silent Ship (Exploration)**
The crew must board the Kaelen freighter, the *Rust-Bucket*. The ship is dark, cold, and gravity is offline.
*   **Hazard:** Zero-Gravity (Agility rolls to maneuver).
*   **Discovery:** The customs team is dead, their bodies perfectly preserved but their eyes turned solid black. A Difficulty 12 Knowledge roll reveals this is not a known biological or chemical weapon.
*   **The Artifact:** In the shielded cargo hold, the crew finds a geometric, crystalline void-shape (an Eclipse artifact) humming with dark energy.

**Encounter 2: The Syndicate Ambush (Combat)**
As the crew secures the artifact, a Kaelen Syndicate boarding party breaches the airlock, intending to reclaim their cargo.
*   **Adversaries:** 3x **Syndicate Enforcers** (Tier 1 Minions), 1x **Syndicate Fixer** (Tier 1 Support).
*   **Tactics:** The Fixer stays in the corridor, using *Mark the Target* while the Enforcers push into the zero-G cargo hold.

**The Choice & Consequences:**
The crew has the artifact. Who gets it?
*   **Give it to Vance (Terran Coalition):** Gain +1 Reputation with the Coalition. Vance pays the crew 2,000 Credits. The Coalition begins secretly studying the artifact, which will have disastrous consequences in Arc 2.
*   **Sell it to the Syndicate:** Gain +1 Reputation with the Kaelen Syndicate, lose -1 with the Coalition. The Syndicate pays 4,000 Credits. The Syndicate weaponizes the artifact, making their ships deadlier in Arc 2.
*   **Destroy it:** The artifact explodes, dealing 2d8 magic damage to anyone in Close range. The crew gains no money, but the Vanguard operative Lyra contacts them, impressed by their wisdom.

---

sections.title: Chapter 2: The Mind's Eye
sections.content:
**Level:** 2
**Featured Factions:** The Mindclave, Aurelian Empire, The Vanguard

**The Hook:**
A prominent Aurelian diplomat is found murdered in the Blue Ring, his mind wiped clean. The Mindclave immediately takes jurisdiction, but the Vanguard suspects a deeper conspiracy involving rogue telepaths and Aurelian precognition.

> *"The Grand Assembly Hall is a masterpiece of Aurelian architecture—sweeping arches of gold and crimson. Today, it is a crime scene. Ambassador Valerius lies on the marble floor, untouched by any physical weapon, but his eyes are vacant, his mind entirely erased. A Mindclave Hound stands over the body, a telepathic dampener humming on their belt. From the shadows of a nearby pillar, a woman with a Star-Sword pin catches your eye and gestures for you to follow her."*

**Encounter 1: The Vanguard's Plea (Social)**
The woman is Lyra, a Vanguard operative. She explains that Valerius was having precognitive visions of the Eclipse returning, and the Mindclave silenced him to prevent a panic. She needs the crew to break into the Xenobiology Lab to retrieve Valerius's personal data-pad before the Mindclave destroys it.
*   **Challenge:** Difficulty 14 Presence or Deception roll to bypass the Aurelian guards outside the lab without causing an incident.

**Encounter 2: The Mindclave Intercept (Combat/Skulk)**
Inside the lab, the crew finds the data-pad, but they are ambushed by a Mindclave hit squad sent to scrub the evidence.
*   **Adversaries:** 2x **Mindclave Hounds** (Tier 1 Skulks), 1x **Mindclave Inquisitor** (Tier 2 Support).
*   **Tactics:** The Hounds use *Surface Scan* to prevent the crew from hiding, while the Inquisitor uses *Mind Spike* to break the crew's heaviest hitter.

**The Choice & Consequences:**
The data-pad contains Valerius's visions: a massive, crystalline fleet destroying Nexus 9.
*   **Give the data to Lyra (Vanguard):** Gain +1 Reputation with the Vanguard. Lyra uses the data to begin preparing a defense. The Mindclave marks the crew as enemies.
*   **Give the data to Director Thorne (Mindclave):** Gain +1 Reputation with the Mindclave, lose -1 with the Vanguard. Thorne pays the crew handsomely (3,000 Credits) and assures them the "rogue telepath problem" is handled.

---

sections.title: Chapter 3: The Siege of the Warrens
sections.content:
**Level:** 2
**Featured Factions:** Terran Coalition, Kaelen Syndicate

**The Hook:**
A labor dispute in the Grey Ring escalates into a full-blown riot, fueled by Kaelen agitators and Terran Coalition heavy-handedness. The crew is trapped in the Warrens and must negotiate a ceasefire before Coalition Security deploys lethal force.

> *"The Grey Ring is burning. The air scrubbers have failed, and the smell of smoke and sweat is suffocating. Barricades of scrap metal and overturned cargo haulers block the main transit spoke. On one side, hundreds of desperate workers, armed with mining lasers and Kaelen-supplied scrap-pistols. On the other, a phalanx of Terran Coalition riot police, weapons drawn. You are caught in the middle, and the countdown to a massacre has begun."*

**Encounter 1: The Agitator (Social/Investigation)**
The crew must find the Kaelen agitator who is whipping the crowd into a frenzy. A Difficulty 13 Investigation or Instinct roll locates him: a Syndicate Breacher named Kaelen-Tor.
*   **Challenge:** The crew must convince Kaelen-Tor to stand down (Difficulty 15 Presence) or quietly neutralize him without the crowd seeing (Difficulty 14 Agility/Stealth).

**Encounter 2: The Breach (Combat)**
Regardless of the crew's success with the agitator, a squad of automated security drones malfunctions (or is hacked) and opens fire on the crowd. The crew must destroy the drones to prevent a slaughter.
*   **Adversaries:** 4x **Station Security Drones** (Tier 1 Ranged).
*   **Hazard:** Panicking Crowd. At the start of each round, the GM rolls 1d6. On a 1-2, a PC is jostled by the crowd and suffers Disadvantage on their next attack.

**The Choice & Consequences:**
*   **Side with the Workers:** The crew helps the workers hold the barricade. Gain +1 Reputation with the Underbelly/Syndicate. Coalition Security is humiliated, and Commander Vance is furious (-1 Coalition Rep).
*   **Assist Coalition Security:** The crew helps dismantle the barricades and arrest the ringleaders. Gain +1 Reputation with the Coalition. The workers are crushed, and the Grey Ring becomes a powder keg of resentment.

---

sections.title: Chapter 4: The Emissary's Warning
sections.content:
**Level:** 3
**Featured Factions:** The Luminaries, The Valari

**The Hook:**
The Luminary ambassador, The Emissary, requests a private meeting with the crew. It speaks in riddles, warning of a "shadow that falls across the stars" and tasking the players with finding a lost Valari soul-crystal that holds the key to the coming war.

> *"The Emissary's chamber is blindingly bright. The Luminary ambassador is a projection of hard-light, a geometric angel that hurts to look at directly. Its voice echoes in your minds rather than your ears. 'The cycle turns,' it says. 'The dark shapes awaken. The Valari hide a truth in their sanctum, a crystal that remembers the last war. Bring it to the Core. The Nexus must be primed.'"*

**Encounter 1: The Valari Sanctum (Skulk/Social)**
The crew must infiltrate the Valari embassy in the Blue Ring to steal the soul-crystal. They can attempt a stealthy heist (Difficulty 15 Agility) or try to talk their way past the guards by claiming a religious pilgrimage (Difficulty 16 Presence).

**Encounter 2: The Blademaster's Test (Combat)**
As they secure the crystal, they are confronted by the embassy's protector.
*   **Adversary:** 1x **Valari Blademaster** (Tier 3 Solo).
*   **Tactics:** The Blademaster does not want to kill the crew, only test their worthiness. If the crew reduces the Blademaster to half HP, he yields, recognizing that destiny is at work.

**The Choice & Consequences:**
The crystal pulses with a warm, blue light. When touched, it grants a vision of the Nexus Core activating as a massive weapon.
*   **Take the crystal to the Nexus Core:** The crew plugs the crystal into the station's central hub. The station shudders, and a massive energy shield briefly flickers to life around Nexus 9. The Emissary nods. The station is now fortified for Arc 2.
*   **Return the crystal to the Valari:** The crew respects Valari sovereignty and returns the artifact. Gain +2 Reputation with the Valari. The Valari promise their fleet's aid in the coming war, but Nexus 9 remains unshielded.

*(End of Arc 1)*

---

chapters.content: The first arc establishes the setting, introduces the major factions, and hints at the deeper mysteries of the galaxy. The players are drawn into the political web of Nexus 9, dealing with smugglers, rogue telepaths, and the simmering tensions between the Aurelian Empire and the Kaelen Syndicate.

chapters.title: ARC 2: THE SHADOWS LENGTHEN (Levels 4-6)

sections.title: Chapter 5: The Gilded Trap
sections.content:
**Level:** 4
**Featured Factions:** Aurelian Empire, The Eclipse

**The Hook:**
The crew is invited to a lavish gala on an Aurelian core world, ostensibly to negotiate a trade agreement. In reality, they are walking into a trap set by The Whisperer, who seeks to corrupt or eliminate them.

> *"Aurelia Prime is a world of impossible wealth. The Imperial Conclave is a floating palace of gold and glass, suspended above a sapphire ocean. You are dressed in your finest, surrounded by nobles in silk and bioluminescent jewelry. But the music is too loud, the smiles are too sharp, and standing by the Emperor's throne is a man in a perfectly tailored black suit. He catches your eye, raises a glass, and mouths a single word: 'Welcome.'"*

**Encounter 1: The Whisperer's Offer (Social)**
The man in black is The Whisperer, the primary agent of the Eclipse. He approaches the crew during the gala and offers them power, wealth, or the fulfillment of their deepest desires if they agree to sabotage Nexus 9's defenses.
*   **Challenge:** The crew must resist his telepathic manipulation (Difficulty 16 Presence) while maintaining their composure in front of the Imperial Court.

**Encounter 2: The Assassination Attempt (Combat)**
When the crew refuses (or if they accept but The Whisperer decides they are too dangerous), the trap is sprung. The gala's entertainment—a troupe of acrobats—reveals themselves as Eclipse-Touched assassins.
*   **Adversaries:** 4x **Void-Callers** (Tier 2 Leaders), 1x **Aurelian Centurion** (Tier 2 Leader, corrupted by the Eclipse).
*   **Hazard:** Panicking Nobles. The room is filled with screaming aristocrats. Any ranged attack that misses its target by 5 or more hits a bystander, causing a massive diplomatic incident (-2 Aurelian Rep).

**The Choice & Consequences:**
The crew escapes the gala, but they have proof that the Emperor is being manipulated by the Eclipse.
*   **Expose the Emperor:** The crew broadcasts the evidence to the Aurelian nobility. The Empire falls into a brutal civil war, removing them as a threat but plunging a third of the galaxy into chaos.
*   **Keep the Secret:** The crew uses the evidence to blackmail the Emperor into withdrawing his fleet from the frontier. The Empire remains intact, but The Whisperer's influence grows stronger in the shadows.

---

sections.title: Chapter 6: The Blood Oath
sections.content:
**Level:** 5
**Featured Factions:** Kaelen Syndicate, Aurelian Empire

**The Hook:**
The Kaelen Syndicate, provoked by Aurelian aggression, declares a Ven'Kari (blood oath) against the entire Aurelian royal family. The crew must intervene to prevent a massacre that would plunge the galaxy into total war.

> *"The Scorch is a dead world, its atmosphere burned away by centuries of Aurelian orbital bombardment. The Kaelen Revolutionary Council meets in a subterranean bunker, lit by the harsh glare of forge-fires. Warlord Kael stands before the assembly, a jagged scar across his face. He raises a rusted blade and slices his own palm. 'The Ven'Kari is sworn,' he roars. 'The Aurelian bloodline ends today.' The crowd erupts in a deafening chant of war."*

**Encounter 1: The Trial by Combat (Combat)**
To stop the Kaelen fleet from launching, the crew must invoke an ancient Syndicate law and challenge Warlord Kael's champion to a duel in the fighting pits.
*   **Adversary:** 1x **Syndicate "Cleaner"** (Tier 3 Skulk) or 1x **Syndicate Breacher** (Tier 2 Bruiser) depending on the party's composition.
*   **Rules of the Pit:** No energy weapons, no telepathy. Only kinetic weapons and blades. The fight is to the death, or until one side yields.

**Encounter 2: The Aurelian Retaliation (Ship Combat)**
While the crew is in the pits, an Aurelian strike force arrives in orbit to preemptively glass the Kaelen bunker. The crew must rush to their ship and hold off the Aurelians long enough for the Kaelen planetary shields to activate.
*   **Adversaries:** 2x Aurelian Frigates (use Tier 2 Ship stats).
*   **Objective:** Survive for 4 rounds while the Kaelen shields power up.

**The Choice & Consequences:**
The crew has stopped the immediate massacre, but the Ven'Kari remains.
*   **Broker a Truce:** The crew convinces Kael to target only the Emperor, not the entire bloodline. The Kaelen fleet launches a surgical strike, weakening the Empire but avoiding total war.
*   **Let the Kaelen Burn:** The crew abandons the Kaelen to the Aurelian bombardment. The Syndicate is crippled, but the Aurelian Empire becomes an unstoppable juggernaut, fully controlled by the Eclipse.

---

sections.title: Chapter 7: The Silent Fleet
sections.content:
**Level:** 5
**Featured Factions:** The Vanguard, Terran Coalition

**The Hook:**
The crew discovers a derelict fleet of ancient Vanguard warships hidden in a nebula near the Valari border. They must reactivate the ships and secure them before the Eclipse or the Terran Coalition can claim them.

> *"The nebula is a swirling mass of violet and bruised purple gas. Your sensors are blind, relying entirely on visual feeds. And then, you see them. Dozens of massive, angular warships, their hulls scarred by weapons fire from a war fought a thousand years ago. They are dead, silent, and drifting. But as your ship approaches, a single, faint beacon pulses from the flagship. Someone—or something—is still alive in there."*

**Encounter 1: The Ghost Ship (Exploration/Skulk)**
The crew boards the Vanguard flagship, the *Aethelgard*. The ship is a labyrinth of dead corridors and failing life support.
*   **Hazard:** Radiation Leaks (1 Fear to trigger, 1 Stress per turn).
*   **Discovery:** The crew finds the ship's AI core, which contains the coordinates of the Eclipse stronghold, The Hollow.

**Encounter 2: The Coalition Boarding Party (Combat)**
A Terran Coalition stealth cruiser, tracking the crew's warp signature, arrives and deploys a boarding party to seize the ancient technology.
*   **Adversaries:** 1x **Aurelian Praetorian Guard** (Tier 3 Solo, reskinned as a Terran Special Forces Heavy), 3x **Aurelian Legionnaires** (Tier 1 Standards, reskinned as Terran Marines).
*   **Tactics:** The Terrans use heavy armor and disciplined fire to push the crew away from the AI core.

**The Choice & Consequences:**
The crew has the coordinates to The Hollow and control of the derelict fleet.
*   **Give the Fleet to the Coalition:** The Terrans gain a massive technological advantage. They promise to lead the assault on The Hollow, but their authoritarian government uses the ships to oppress the frontier first.
*   **Give the Fleet to the Vanguard:** Lyra and her hidden operatives take command of the ships. The Vanguard becomes a major military power, ready to strike at the Eclipse, but the Coalition brands the crew as traitors.

---

sections.title: Chapter 8: The Fall of Nexus 9
sections.content:
**Level:** 6
**Featured Factions:** All major factions, The Eclipse

**The Hook:**
The Eclipse launch a devastating surprise attack on Nexus 9, seeking to destroy the diplomatic hub and claim the Nexus Core. The crew must coordinate the station's defense, evacuate civilians, and face The Whisperer in a final showdown.

> *"The klaxons are screaming. Red emergency lighting bathes the Promenade in a bloody glow. Outside the massive viewport, the stars are blotted out by geometric, crystalline shapes—Eclipse void-ships, tearing through the station's defensive perimeter. Commander Vance's voice crackles over the comms, barely audible over the sound of hull breaches. 'They're inside the perimeter. They're going for the Core. God help us all.'"*

**Encounter 1: The Evacuation of the Warrens (Skill Challenge)**
The lower decks are venting atmosphere. The crew must guide hundreds of panicking civilians to the escape pods while fighting off Eclipse boarding parties.
*   **Challenge:** A 6-segment progress clock. The crew must accumulate 6 successful skill checks (Agility, Presence, Strength) before the GM accumulates 4 Fear tokens. Failure means massive civilian casualties.

**Encounter 2: The Whisperer at the Core (Boss Combat)**
The crew reaches the central hub just as The Whisperer breaches the Nexus Core chamber. He is attempting to corrupt the station's power source.
*   **Adversary:** 1x **The Whisperer** (use **Mindclave High Councilor** stats, Tier 4 Boss, reskinned with void magic).
*   **Hazard:** Core Overload. At the end of every round, the room pulses with raw energy. All PCs must make a Difficulty 15 Agility roll or take 2d8 magic damage.

**The Choice & Consequences:**
The Whisperer is defeated, but the station is critically damaged. The Eclipse fleet is still outside.
*   **Activate the Core's Defense Grid:** The crew uses the Valari soul-crystal (from Chapter 4) to trigger a massive EMP. The Eclipse fleet is disabled, but Nexus 9's life support is fried. The station must be abandoned, becoming a dead monument.
*   **Flee and Let the Station Fall:** The crew escapes with the station's leadership and the Core's data. Nexus 9 is destroyed by the Eclipse, but the crew's ship is fully loaded with refugees and critical intelligence for the final war.

*(End of Arc 2)*

---

chapters.content: The second arc escalates the conflict. The Eclipse begin to move openly, manipulating the Aurelian Empire into a war of expansion. The players must forge alliances, uncover ancient secrets, and survive the growing chaos.

chapters.title: ARC 3: THE CRUCIBLE OF STARS (Levels 7-10)

sections.title: Chapter 9: The Broken Alliance
sections.content:
**Level:** 7
**Featured Factions:** Terran Coalition, The Mindclave, The Valari

**The Hook:**
In the aftermath of the attack on Nexus 9, the Terran Coalition and the Valari are on the brink of war, blaming each other for the station's vulnerability. The crew must mediate a desperate peace summit while fending off Mindclave assassins.

> *"Earth is a fortress world, its skies choked with orbital defense platforms. The peace summit is held in the Geneva Spire, a towering monument to Terran bureaucracy. The Valari delegation sits on one side of the circular table, their armor gleaming. The Terran generals sit on the other, their faces hard. The air is thick with accusations. And then, the lights flicker, and the Mindclave makes its move."*

**Encounter 1: The Summit Ambush (Combat)**
Director Thorne of the Mindclave, having secretly allied with the Eclipse, sends a hit squad to assassinate both delegations and plunge the galaxy into chaos.
*   **Adversaries:** 2x **Mindclave Handlers** (Tier 3 Leaders), 4x **Mindclave Hounds** (Tier 2 Skulks).
*   **Tactics:** The Handlers use *Dominate* to turn the Terran and Valari guards against each other, creating a chaotic, three-way firefight.

**Encounter 2: The Revelation (Social/Investigation)**
After the assassins are defeated, the crew finds a data-spike on one of the Handlers. A Difficulty 16 Knowledge roll decrypts it, revealing Thorne's treason and a shocking secret: the Valari surrendered in the last war because their souls resonate with the Eclipse.
*   **Challenge:** The crew must present this evidence to the Terran generals (Difficulty 17 Presence) without causing the Valari to walk out in shame.

**The Choice & Consequences:**
The crew has exposed Thorne and the Valari secret.
*   **Execute Thorne:** The crew tracks down Thorne and kills him. The Mindclave fractures, removing them as a threat but also losing their telepathic support for the final battle.
*   **Blackmail Thorne:** The crew uses the evidence to force Thorne to commit the Mindclave's telepaths to the war effort. The alliance holds, but Thorne remains a dangerous wildcard.

---

sections.title: Chapter 10: The Technoseer's Bargain
sections.content:
**Level:** 8
**Featured Factions:** The Technoseers, The Umbral Covenant

**The Hook:**
To defeat the Eclipse, the crew needs the knowledge of the Technoseers. They must travel to the uncharted Rimward Marches and negotiate with the secretive order, offering something of equal value in exchange for the secrets of Eclipse technology.

> *"The Rimward Marches are a graveyard of dead stars and rogue planets. The Technoseer enclave is built into the hollowed-out core of a shattered moon. The air hums with the sound of a billion calculations per second. The Grand Magus, a being more machine than flesh, floats down from a web of fiber-optic cables. 'You seek the key to The Hollow,' it says, its voice a chorus of synthetic tones. 'But what are you willing to trade for the survival of your species?'"*

**Encounter 1: The Trial of Logic (Puzzle/Social)**
The Technoseers do not value money or political power. They value unique data, rare technology, or profound secrets.
*   **Challenge:** The crew must offer something of immense value (e.g., the Valari soul-crystal, the Vanguard AI core, or a PC's most guarded secret). The GM sets the Difficulty based on the narrative weight of the offering (Difficulty 14-18 Presence or Knowledge).

**Encounter 2: The Umbral Ambush (Combat)**
A splinter faction of Technoseers, the Umbral Covenant, believes the Eclipse should be allowed to cleanse the galaxy. They attack the crew to prevent the exchange.
*   **Adversaries:** 1x **Progenitor Sentinel** (Tier 3 Bruiser, controlled by the Covenant), 3x **Void-Stalkers** (Tier 2 Skulks).
*   **Hazard:** Gravity Fluctuations. The moon's artificial gravity fails randomly. At the start of each round, roll 1d6. On a 1-3, all movement is halved.

**The Choice & Consequences:**
The crew secures the schematics for an Eclipse-breaching weapon, but the cost is high.
*   **Sacrifice a Secret:** A PC gives up a vital piece of their backstory or a powerful artifact. The Technoseers provide the weapon, and the PC permanently loses access to that narrative or mechanical advantage.
*   **Sacrifice a Ship:** The crew trades their own starship (or a significant portion of their fleet). They gain the weapon, but their mobility and firepower are severely reduced for the final assault.

---

sections.title: Chapter 11: The Descent into Darkness
sections.content:
**Level:** 9
**Featured Factions:** All major factions, The Eclipse

**The Hook:**
The grand coalition fleet, led by the players, launches a desperate assault on The Hollow. The crew must navigate the treacherous hyperspace currents, breach the Eclipse defenses, and deliver the Nexus Core to the heart of the enemy stronghold.

> *"The Hollow is not a planet. It is a Dyson sphere made of black, jagged crystal, pulsing with a sickly violet light. Around it, the Eclipse fleet swarms like angry hornets. Your coalition fleet—Terran dreadnoughts, Valari cruisers, Kaelen raiders, and Vanguard stealth ships—drops out of hyperspace. The comms channel is a cacophony of battle cries and terrified screams. 'This is it,' Commander Vance's voice cuts through the static. 'Punch a hole. We'll hold the line.'"*

**Encounter 1: The Fleet Engagement (Ship Combat)**
The crew's ship must lead the spearhead through the Eclipse defensive perimeter.
*   **Adversaries:** 1x **Progenitor Leviathan** (Tier 4 Boss, reskinned as an Eclipse Capital Ship), 4x Eclipse Fighters (use Tier 2 Ship stats).
*   **Objective:** The crew must survive for 5 rounds and destroy the Leviathan to create a breach in the sphere's outer shell.

**Encounter 2: The Boarding Action (Combat)**
The crew's ship crashes through the breach and lands inside The Hollow. They must fight their way to the central chamber carrying the Nexus Core (or the Technoseer weapon).
*   **Adversaries:** 2x **Void Avatars** (Tier 4 Bosses, acting as gatekeepers).
*   **Hazard:** Psychic Onslaught. The Eclipse hive-mind attacks the crew's sanity. Every round, all PCs must make a Difficulty 16 Instinct roll or take 1d8 Stress.

**The Choice & Consequences:**
The crew has breached the inner sanctum, but the coalition fleet outside is being decimated.
*   **Call for Reinforcements:** The crew orders the fleet to push inside the sphere to support them. The crew gains Advantage on all rolls in the final chapter, but the coalition fleet suffers catastrophic, irreplaceable losses.
*   **Hold the Line:** The crew orders the fleet to stay outside and draw fire. The fleet survives, but the crew must face the final boss alone, with no backup and Disadvantage on their first round of attacks.

---

sections.title: Chapter 12: The Dawn of a New Age
sections.content:
**Level:** 10
**Featured Factions:** The Eclipse, The Luminaries

**The Hook:**
The final confrontation. The crew must detonate the Nexus Core while holding off the Eclipse leadership and their Keepers. The fate of the galaxy rests on their actions, and survival is not guaranteed.

> *"The Inner Sanctum is a vast, echoing chamber of obsidian and starlight. At its center floats the Eclipse Overmind—a swirling vortex of dark energy and screaming souls. The Whisperer stands before it, his physical form burning away as he channels the Overmind's power. 'You are too late,' he hisses, his voice echoing in your skulls. 'The cycle ends. The silence begins.' You drop the Nexus Core onto the dais. The countdown starts. You have three minutes to save the galaxy."*

**Encounter 1: The Overmind (Final Boss Combat)**
This is the ultimate battle. The crew must survive while the Nexus Core charges for detonation.
*   **Adversary:** 1x **The Eclipse Overmind** (Tier 4 Legendary Boss).
    *   *HP:* 50 | *Evasion:* 16 | *Armor:* 5
    *   *Major Threshold:* 25 | *Severe Threshold:* 45
    *   *Attacks:* Void Tendril (Presence, Far, 3d10+5 Magic), Reality Tear (Instinct, Close, 4d8+5 Physical).
    *   *Fear Moves:* **Consume Hope (2 Fear):** The Overmind drains 1 Hope from every PC and heals 1d10 HP. **Summon the Swarm (3 Fear):** Spawns 4x Void-Spawn minions.

**Encounter 2: The Detonation (Skill Challenge/Sacrifice)**
The Core reaches critical mass, but the automated trigger is damaged by the Overmind's attacks. Someone must stay behind to manually detonate it.
*   **Challenge:** A Difficulty 20 Knowledge or Agility roll to repair the trigger remotely. If the roll fails, a PC (or a beloved NPC) must sacrifice themselves to trigger the blast.

**The Choice & Consequences:**
The Core detonates, shattering The Hollow and banishing the Eclipse back into the void.
*   **The Ultimate Sacrifice:** A PC stays behind. They die a hero's death, their name etched into the stars. The rest of the crew escapes as The Hollow collapses.
*   **The Miraculous Escape:** The crew manages to repair the trigger and flee. They survive, but the galaxy is forever changed by the war.

**Epilogue:**
The GM narrates the aftermath based on the crew's choices throughout the campaign. Did the Aurelian Empire fall? Did the Kaelen Syndicate find legitimacy? Is Nexus 9 a rebuilt beacon of hope, or a floating graveyard? The players describe what their surviving characters do next in the new, fragile peace they have forged.

*(End of Campaign)*

chapters.content: The final arc brings the campaign to its climax. The galaxy is at war, the Eclipse are fully awakened, and the players must unite the fractured factions to make a final stand at The Hollow.

chapters.title: Definitions
chapters.content:
- **Licensed Content**: (a) game rules (but NOT copy/paste of rulebook content), mechanics, systems, toolsets, reference cards, and stat blocks; and (b) the names and attributes of game characters, settings, locations, monsters, classes, subclasses, and races featured in DRP Games. EXCLUDES all Prohibited Content.
- **Prohibited Content**: (a) trademarks, logos, trade dress, trade names, and service marks; (b) videos, trailers, audio sounds, music, video games, comic books, novels, artwork, visual images, illustrations, drawings, graphics, imagery, plots, storylines, published work titles, and maps; (c) the exact text of manuals, guides, handbooks, Campaign Frames, and rulebooks; and (d) DRP Published Content, unless identified as Public Game Content.
- **Public Game Content**: Licensed Content AND any additional content explicitly identified by DRP as Public Game Content. Currently: Daggerheart System Reference Document 1.0 (including Domain icons) and Candela Obscura.
- **Adaptive Content**: content derived from or based on Public Game Content in which the Public Game Content is translated, altered, rearranged, transformed, or otherwise modified.

chapters.title: Key Takeaway
chapters.content:
- We CAN use: game rules, mechanics, systems, stat block formats, Domain names, class structure concepts FROM THE SRD
- We CANNOT: copy/paste exact rulebook text, use their artwork, use their plots/storylines, use their trade dress
- We CAN create "Adaptive Content" = content derived from the SRD that we alter/transform
- The SRD text itself is Public Game Content and can be used as a base

chapters.title: Section 2: Scope
chapters.content:
**2.1 License Grant:** DRP grants a worldwide, royalty-free, non-transferable, non-exclusive license to:
(a) reproduce and Share the Public Game Content in whole or in part
(b) produce, reproduce, Share and SELL Adaptive Content using the Public Game Content solely in Permitted Formats

**Key: We CAN sell Adaptive Content.** The SRD is Public Game Content. Our sci-fi adaptations are Adaptive Content.

chapters.title: Section 2.5: Use of Name Marks
chapters.content:
- Name Marks CANNOT be used in the title of a work or a chapter title
- Name Marks CANNOT appear on the front cover of any work
- Name Marks MUST include "Compatible" adjacent to it in marketing and descriptive text
- So we can say "Compatible with Daggerheart" but NOT "Daggerheart: Nexus 9" as a title

chapters.title: Section 4: Attribution
chapters.content:
If we Share Public Game Content or Adaptive Content, we must include:
(a) copyright notice
(b) attribution statement identifying DRP as creator
(c) hyperlink to Public Game Content
(d) statement that content is licensed under DPCGL
(e) statement indicating whether we modified the Public Game Content

chapters.title: CRITICAL CONCLUSION FOR NEXUS 9:
chapters.content:
1. We CAN reuse the mechanical STRUCTURE from the SRD (2d12 system, Hope/Fear, Domains, action economy, damage thresholds, leveling structure)
2. We CAN create Adaptive Content (our own classes, weapons, Domain cards) that follow the SRD's mechanical patterns
3. We CANNOT copy/paste exact SRD text — we must rewrite in our own words
4. We CAN sell the final product
5. We must include proper attribution
6. "Daggerheart" cannot be in our title or on our cover — only in "Compatible with Daggerheart" descriptive text

---

chapters.title: Appendix B: Telepathy Quick Reference

sections.title: The P-Rating Scale
sections.content:
Telepaths are rated on a 12-point scale (P1 to P12) by the Mindclave.

| Rating | Capability | Mechanical Equivalent |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **P1 - P3** | Surface empathy, sensing strong emotions, detecting lies. | Can roll Presence to read a room or detect deception. |
| **P4 - P6** | Surface thoughts, projecting short messages, mental static. | Can use basic Arcana cards (Level 1-3). |
| **P7 - P9** | Deep memory extraction, psychic attacks, mind-wipes. | Can use advanced Arcana cards (Level 4-7). |
| **P10 - P12** | Complete cognitive override, mass illusions, psychic screams. | Can use master Arcana cards (Level 8-10). |

sections.title: Telepathic Resistance
sections.content:
When a telepath attempts to read or manipulate an unwilling target, it triggers a **Psychic Contest**.

1. The telepath makes an Action Roll (usually Presence or Knowledge).
2. The target's Difficulty is their **Evasion + 2** (representing mental fortitude).
3. If the target is also a telepath, they can spend 1 Stress to increase their Difficulty by +2 for that roll.

sections.title: The Cost of Power
sections.content: Pushing telepathic abilities beyond safe limits causes physical trauma. A GM can offer a telepath a **Devil's Bargain**: "You can automatically succeed on this deep scan, but you must mark 2 Stress and take a Minor Scar (Nosebleed/Migraine)."

chapters.content: Telepathy in *Nexus 9* is not magic; it is a forced evolutionary trait that allows a user to perceive and manipulate the quantum states of other minds. While Mystics and Brokers use the Arcana domain to represent their trained abilities, any character with the "Telepath" Experience or a Mindclave background can attempt basic telepathic feats.
